name: composition_advisor
purpose: JSON knowledge base + decision rules consulted before generating MIDI patterns. Encodes music theory primitives, genre-specific conventions, energy/frequency-space allocations, silence protocols, and conditional 'good->WOW' branches drawn from observed failure modes of prior attempts (Faust, Nemesis, Bramure, Venin).

core_principles.rule: A drop only hits if the bar before STARVES the ear. Build hunger before payoff.
core_principles.concrete_test: Last bar before Drop must have ≥ 1 element (drum, bass, or harmony) drop out OR thin to single track for ≥ 2 beats.

core_principles.rule: Silence is the 4th compositional dimension after pitch/duration/timbre. Plant it deliberately, not by omission.
core_principles.concrete_test: Every section ≥ 8 bars contains at least one PLANNED silence (sub-bar to multi-bar) at a structural pivot.

core_principles.rule: A Final Drop is more memorable when it RESTATES something from earlier (motif, single note, rhythmic idea) than when it adds new layers.
core_principles.concrete_test: Final Drop must contain ≥ 1 callback (intro_drone, verse_motif octave-up, bridge_chord_resolution, or rhythmic quote).

core_principles.rule: Each section carries exactly ONE compositional idea (rhythmic OR melodic OR harmonic OR textural). Stacking ideas crowds; isolating one makes it audible.
core_principles.concrete_test: Identify each section's PRIMARY idea in 1 sentence. If multiple compete, simplify.

core_principles.rule: When ≥ 2 instruments share a critical band, exactly ONE leads; the other yields (-6 dB OR pan ≥ 40% off-center OR sidechain duck).
core_principles.concrete_test: Audit by band: at most 1 dominant per [sub, low, low-mid, mid, high-mid, high, air].

core_principles.rule: Velocity curves > velocity steps. Timing offsets ±1/64 humanize. Pitch wobble (±10 cents) adds desperate/menacing quality. Mathematically perfect = sterile.
core_principles.concrete_test: No pattern should use a single velocity value across all hits of one instrument in one section.

philosophy.good_to_wow_meta: Each conditional branch in this API encodes ONE specific lift from 'sounds OK' to 'memorable'. Apply branches conservatively — one well-executed WOW per section beats five competing ones.

voicing_recipes.i_sus2: root + 2 + 5 — leaves the b3 implicit, dark but unresolved
voicing_recipes.VI_open: b6 + 1 + b3 — wide open, classic Aeolian
voicing_recipes.VII_to_i: VII major resolves down a whole step to i — the Aeolian cadence

aeolian.characteristic_dissonance: VII_to_i (Aeolian cadence is itself the tension — feels modal, not Classical-tonal)

chord_families.ii: minor (the natural 6 lives here as the chord's 5)
chord_families.IV: major (b3, 6, 1) — the SIGNATURE Dorian chord, lifted by raised-6

voicing_recipes.i_to_IV: the i->IV move is the Dorian payoff — the raised 6 is suddenly audible
voicing_recipes.i_add6: root + b3 + 5 + 6 — Dorian without committing to chord IV

use_for: verse that wants relief without resolution
dorian.characteristic_dissonance: natural 6 against b3 (a major 4th interval that shouldn't exist in pure minor) — the Dorian raised-6 tension

signature_intervals: b2 (the half-step ABOVE the root — primary tension)

chord_families.bII: major (b2, 4, b6) — the SIGNATURE Phrygian chord, tritone-laden push from i

voicing_recipes.i_with_b9: root + b3 + 5 + b9 (the b2 pitched up an octave) — root + immediate dissonance
voicing_recipes.i_to_bII: the half-step UP move from i to bII is the Phrygian signature — sounds Spanish/flamenco/menacing
voicing_recipes.i_sus_b2: root + b2 + 5 — sus voicing where the b2 is the suspension

phrygian.characteristic_dissonance: b2 against root = minor 2nd, the primary musical 'wrongness' interval. Use sparingly or mode loses identity.

phrygian_dominant.scale_name: Phrygian Dominant (5th mode of harmonic minor)
signature_intervals: MAJOR 3 (the maj-3 over b2 = aug-2 interval = snake-charmer)

chord_families.I: major (1, 3, 5) — surprise major chord on the dark mode

voicing_recipes.I_dominant7_no5: root + maj3 + b7 — the venom voicing, no 5 lets the maj3-vs-b7 tritone dominate
voicing_recipes.I_sus4: root + 4 + 5 — avoid committing the maj-3 (lets bass deliver it)

phrygian_dominant.characteristic_dissonance: aug-2 interval between b2 and maj-3 (D to F nat in C# Phr Dom) — the unmistakable signature.

signature_intervals: b5 (the diminished 5th — root chord is i°, NO stable consonance)

chord_families.i_dim: diminished — the root chord itself is unstable

voicing_recipes.i_dim_no5: root + b3 — pseudo-stable by hiding the b5
voicing_recipes.i_with_b5: root + b3 + b5 — full diminished, maximum instability

locrian.characteristic_dissonance: Locrian has NO stable tonic — the root is itself a tritone away from the b5. Treat as a passing flavor, not a home key. Used wholesale = listener loses anchor.
locrian.warning: Per user preference (less dissonance), prefer Phrygian or Phrygian Dominant instead of Locrian for most use cases.

signature_intervals: natural 7 (leading tone — this is what makes it 'harmonic')

chord_families.V: MAJOR (5, 7, 2) — the SIGNATURE chord, contains the leading tone — Classical V-i cadence

voicing_recipes.V_to_i: the dominant V to tonic i resolution — feels classical but with the b6 lurking
voicing_recipes.i_add_nat7: root + b3 + 5 + nat7 — minor-major 7 chord, very brooding
voicing_recipes.iv_to_V_to_i: ii->V->i but with both ii dim and V major — the harmonic minor cadence

avoid_for: modal (use Aeolian/Dorian instead) — Harmonic Minor pulls toward classical resolution
harmonic_minor.characteristic_dissonance: the aug-2 between b6 and nat-7 — exoticism that resolves cleanly via the leading tone.

voice_leading.purpose: How voices (instruments treated as melodic lines) move in relation to each other. Modern grunge/industrial breaks classical rules deliberately — these rules document both the conventions AND the genre exceptions, so the advisor can grant exception permits with intent.

core_rules.rule: Inner voices (pad chord tones, counter-leads, sustained synths) should move by step (m2 or M2) when possible. Leaps > minor 3rd in inner voices sound jarring.

exceptions.reason: octave displacement on bass = power move, not voice-leading violation
lead jumping octave UP at section peak amplifies climax

core_rules.rule: Two voices moving in PARALLEL 5ths or octaves between chord changes sounds 'thin' or 'primitive' in classical writing.

exceptions.reason: Cobain genre — root+5 IS the chord, parallel motion is the GENRE
doubling the lead in octaves is standard production
modern modal voicings move in 4ths/5ths intentionally

core_rules.rule: When changing chords, keep any shared note in the SAME voice (don't move to a different instrument). Reduces motion, preserves continuity.
core_rules.wow_lift: smoothest chord changes the listener barely perceives — 'glue'

core_rules.rule: When outer voices move opposite directions (bass UP while top voice DOWN, or inverse), the harmony feels grounded and inevitable.
core_rules.wow_lift: cadential motion sounds like resolution even when modal

core_rules.rule: In Harmonic Minor, the natural 7 (leading tone) MUST resolve UP to root within 2 beats. Otherwise the cadence breaks.
core_rules.violation_penalty: loss of cadential pull — chord progression sounds aimless

core_rules.rule: Drone voices NEVER move within a section. If a drone must change, do it AT THE SECTION BOUNDARY by sliding a half-step (chromatic shift, no bigger).
core_rules.wow_lift: the half-step drone shift at a boundary signals 'something has changed' subliminally

between_instruments.bass_vs_pad: Bass moves while pad holds (or inverse) — never both move on same beat except at section change. Reduces clash, preserves clarity.
between_instruments.lead_vs_counter_lead: If lead moves UP by step, counter-lead moves DOWN by step (contrary motion = independence). If both move parallel, they fuse into one voice.
between_instruments.drone_role: Drone is the SPINE — every other voice references it. Moving voices should land on drone-compatible chord tones at downbeats.
between_instruments.kick_vs_sub_bass: Kick LOCKS pitch with sub-bass on downbeats. Sub-bass holds pitch BETWEEN kicks (no clash). Ducking sub-bass on kick attack is OPTIONAL but recommended for clarity.
between_instruments.vocal_vs_lead_synth: Vocal occupies 200-3kHz primarily. If lead synth shares this band, lead synth must drop -3 dB OR pan ≥ 30% off-center when vocal is present.

anti_patterns_voice_leading: All voices moving in parallel motion → sounds like one instrument doubled
Bass and pad both moving on every beat → muddy, no anchor
Lead and counter-lead doing the same melodic shape an interval apart → no independence, defeats the counter-lead purpose
Drone changing pitch mid-section → breaks the spine

tension_release.purpose: Cadences and tension techniques — how to build expectation and either deliver or subvert it. The 'WOW' lift comes from CHOOSING when to resolve vs surprise.

use_for: Final Drop landing if home key
modes: harmonic_minor (only modal cadence with strong V)
authentic.comment: Sounds 'classical' / safe. Reach for this when you want unmistakable arrival.

modal_aeolian.form: bVII major -> i minor (descending whole step)

deceptive.form: V (build expectation of i) -> VI major (instead)
deceptive.strength: surprise twist — listener expects resolution, gets a major lift somewhere new
use_for: bridge ending that DOESN'T resolve to home
modulation pivot via the unexpected chord
deceptive.comment: Most underused powerful cadence. Use ONCE per song max.

interrupted_silence.form: build chord -> SILENCE (1 to 2 beats) -> resolution chord
interrupted_silence.comment: Combines tension_release WITH silence_protocol — the gap IS the cadence

suspended_unresolved.form: end on sus2 or sus4 (or open quartal voicing), never resolve to triad
use_for: modal hover that intentionally stays unfinished
suspended_unresolved.comment: Anti-classical — leaves the listener thinking the song could continue forever

plagal_modal.form: iv -> i (subdominant -> tonic, no V involvement)
use_for: after a deceptive cadence to soothe

neapolitan.definition: bII major chord built on flat 2 in a minor key (e.g. Db major in C minor)
neapolitan.function: Subdominant substitute with chromatic flavor — in 1st inversion (bII6) is classic, in root position is more modern.
neapolitan.example_in_C_minor: Db major chord — feels like a doorway

chromatic_mediant.definition: Sudden chord shift to a 3rd-related root (bIII, III, bVI, VI) without prep — feels surprising because no common-tone setup.
chromatic_mediant.function: Instant mood pivot WITHOUT modulation (returns to home key after).
chromatic_mediant.comment: Reznor / Yorke signature. The chord drops into the listener like an interruption from another world.

pivot_half_step.definition: Whole arrangement shifts root by minor 2 (up or down). Every voice moves +1 or -1 semitone.
pivot_half_step.function: The 'half-step modulation' shock — most striking in popular music.
use_for: Final Drop ending crash-back DOWN to home
pivot_half_step.duration_recommendation: First 4 bars of the section ONLY, then crash back home; OR sustain the new key to end (commits to it).
pivot_half_step.comment: Used in Nemesis Final Drop. User loved this. Reuse strategically — it loses power if used twice in same song.

tritone_substitution.definition: bII7 replaces V7 — same tritone interval but bass is a half-step above tonic instead of a 5th below.
tritone_substitution.function: Jazz move that ADAPTS to metal — adds chromatic density without losing the V function.
use_for: pre-Drop chord that lands HARD on the Drop
any V-to-i moment that wants more bite

false_relation.definition: Two voices simultaneously play the SAME pitch class natural and altered (e.g. C natural in bass, C# in pad — at the same beat).
false_relation.function: Momentary clash that resolves within 1 beat to stable harmony — Reznor signature dissonance.
use_for: Drop pre-roll for 1 beat of menace
false_relation.comment: Don't sustain — dissonance must clear quickly or it sounds like a mistake.

silence_as_dominant.definition: Replace the V (dominant) chord with literal silence of equivalent length. The expectation of V is so strong that absence ITSELF cadences.
silence_as_dominant.function: Silence borrows the function of V via expectation — Reznor / NIN trick.
use_for: pre-Drop where you'd normally place a sustained V chord
silence_as_dominant.comment: Pairs with interrupted_silence cadence — same idea applied differently

always_resolve: Final Drop entry (the listener earned this)
never_resolve: Outro if Yorke ≥ 25% (ostinato fade > authentic cadence)
Hollow ending (the void has its own logic — resolution would feel cheap)
resolve_with_surprise: Bridge ending → use deceptive cadence (V→VI not V→i)
Drop entry from Pre-Drop → use interrupted_silence (silence + landing)
modulation_resolve: If pivot_half_step UP at Final Drop bars 1-4, resolve back to home with authentic cadence at bar 5
If chromatic_mediant chord introduced, RETURN to home within 4 bars max

anti_patterns_tension: Using authentic cadence at every section boundary → predictable, no impact
Multiple deceptive cadences in same song → loses surprise value
Sustaining false_relation > 1 beat → sounds like a wrong note, not a feature
Modulating up half-step AND down half-step in same song → cancels emotional direction
Silence after silence → silence loses meaning if it's the default

voicings_recipes.purpose: Concrete intervallic recipes for stacking notes. Each recipe has a polyphony cost (n voices), a character description, and pairing rules with other recipes when 2 instruments stack on the same chord. The advisor picks recipes by section context + genre blend, not by name alone.

power_chord.character: wide-open, primal, no commitment to maj/min — root + perfect 5
best_for: any track that wants weight without harmonic specificity
pairs_well_with: drone_over_moving (drone on root, power chord above)

sus2.character: suspended 2 — major-second instead of 3rd, leaves maj/min unresolved
best_for: pad voicings that hold without committing
pairs_well_with: power_chord (sus2 in pad, power in bass)

sus4.character: suspended 4 — perfect 4 instead of 3rd, classical 4-3 suspension when resolved (or held unresolved for modal hover)

drop_2.character: jazz voicing — root, octave, 5, 3 (the 2nd voice from top dropped an octave). Spreads the chord wide.
drop_2.example_in_C_minor: C3 + Eb4 + G3 + C4 (rearranged: C2 + G3 + C3 + Eb3)

quartal.character: built in 4ths instead of 3rds — root + 4 + b7. Modal/modern, no Classical tonal pull
best_for: static harmony that doesn't want to resolve

drone_over_moving.character: sustained low note (drone) with melodic voices above. The drone is pedal point.
best_for: bridge that wants stasis under motion
avoid_for: sections where bass needs to move with chord changes
drone_over_moving.example_in_C_minor: C2 sustained, with C3-Eb3-F3-Eb3 moving above
pairs_well_with: any moving voicing — that's the whole point

first_inversion.character: chord with 3rd in bass — softer than root position, common in voice-led sequences
avoid_for: weight-bearing power moments (root position has more authority)

slash_chord_modal.character: chord with NON-chord-tone bass (e.g., Cm/D) — creates suspended tension
pairs_well_with: any voicing that resolves to home root

min7_no5.character: minor 7 without the 5 — sparse, modern, brooding. Reznor sparse signature.
best_for: verse harmony that wants brood without thickness

open_fifth_dyad.character: JUST root + 5 spread WIDE (root in low octave, 5 nearly 2 octaves up). Maximum width, minimum density.
pairs_well_with: drone_over_moving (the open fifth IS the spine)

vocal_octave_double.character: vocal + lead synth on SAME melody, octave apart — fuses two voices into 'bigger version of vocal'
avoid_for: sections where vocal needs to be exposed alone
vocal_octave_double.example_in_C_minor: vocal at C4, lead synth at C5 (or C3)

chromatic_neighbor_pad.character: root + b9 (chromatic neighbor) + 5 — micro-dissonance held briefly. Reznor false-relation setup.
avoid_for: sustained sections (the b9 will fatigue the ear)
pairs_well_with: resolution to power_chord on next bar
chromatic_neighbor_pad.warning: do NOT sustain >1 bar — sounds like a wrong note instead of a feature

stacking_rules_when_two_instruments_play.principle: Two voicings on the same chord MUST have different intervallic structures so they don't double-up the same notes. The exception is intentional unison/octave doubling.

compatible_pairs.result: the maj-2 floats above the open fifth — Cobain dirty pad
Reznor sparse pad under doubled vocal
Yorke modal hover with pedal bass
lush jazz pad under exposed voice

silence_protocol.purpose: Silence as the 4TH compositional dimension after pitch/duration/timbre. Each level has a wow_factor and explicit placement rules. Silence + cadence pairs are documented because the BIGGEST WOW moments combine both.
silence_protocol.philosophy: Silence is content, not absence. A drop that hits after a planted silence is more memorable than a drop that hits after sound. The advisor should plant silences DELIBERATELY at structural pivots, not by accident.

section_silence.definition: Whole section thinned to 1-2 tracks max — the 'Hollow' section in Venin/Nemesis style. Not literal silence — but everything except 1-2 sustained drone/atmosphere tracks drops out.
placement_rules: Plant ONCE per song — overuse kills its impact
Position right AFTER a major Drop (not before)
The 1-2 surviving tracks must include a sustained drone (continuity reference)
Preceding section ends abruptly (no fade) — the silence IS the transition
section_silence.callback_potential: the surviving drone in section_silence can be CALLED BACK in Final Drop as a fade-in layer (callback through silence)

bar_silence.definition: 1-2 full bars of TOTAL silence in the middle of an otherwise busy section. Most powerful at structural pivots inside drops.
placement_rules: Canonical position: bar 13 of a 16-bar Drop (3/4 through — after enough density to feel the loss)
OR last bar of Pre-Drop into Final Drop (interrupted_silence cadence)
OR bar 7 of an 8-bar Bridge (before the final 8th bar landing)
All tracks cut simultaneously — no fade
The bar AFTER must reenter at maximum density for max impact

beat_silence.definition: 1-2 beats of silence within a bar — usually replacing a hit you'd expect (the listener has been trained on the pattern).
placement_rules: Replace the LAST cluster hit (e.g., 3.75) with silence on EXACTLY 1 bar out of 8 (or 1 out of 4 in shorter sections)
OR replace beat 1 of a bar (the most expected hit) — extreme effect, max 1× per song
Pattern context: must surround the silenced beat with the FULL pattern before and after — the gap must be inside an established pattern
beat_silence.comment: Easy to overdo. If used > 2× per section, the pattern becomes the silence and the silence loses meaning.

subbar_silence.definition: 1/16 to 1/2 beat of silence — micro-gap in a busy 16th-note pattern. Velocity = 0 on specific hits to create breath inside groove.
placement_rules: Apply to hi-hat, arp, or perc patterns — never to kick (kick should always speak)
OR pattern: random 1-in-8 muted for human imperfection
Do NOT mute on downbeats — only on offbeat 16ths
subbar_silence.comment: Subtle — adds groove humanization. NOT a structural surprise but a textural one.

silence_cadence_pairs.principle: The BIGGEST WOW moments combine a tension_release cadence with a silence — the gap IS the cadence's resolution.

pairs.name: interrupted_silence_cadence
pairs.execution: Pre-Drop ends mid-phrase (no resolution). 1 bar of total silence. Final Drop bar 1 entry hits with cluster_density_max.

pairs.name: silence_as_dominant
pairs.context: anywhere a V chord would functionally lead to i
pairs.execution: Replace what would be 2 bars of dominant chord with 2 bars of nothing. The expectation of V is so strong that absence functions as V.

pairs.name: subtle_breath
pairs.execution: After bII chord lands, 1 beat of silence before i resolution. The breath is the 'oh' moment.

pairs.name: groove_breath
pairs.execution: 1-in-8 hi-hat muted across the section. Adds subliminal groove without breaking pattern.

silence_callback_strategy.principle: Silence used in an EARLIER section becomes a callback when used again in a later section.
examples: Hollow (section_silence at midpoint) → fade in Hollow's surviving drone in Final Drop bar 5 → instant emotional payoff
Pre-Drop ends with 1-bar silence → mirror in pre-Final-Drop with 2-bar silence → escalation
Bar-13 silence in Drop 1 → mirror at bar-13 of Final Drop, but EXTENDED to 2 bars → wow

rhythm_theory.purpose: Codifies the rhythmic dimension — polyrhythm, swing, groove feel — so the advisor can pick rhythmic devices that match section energy and genre. Without this, all generated patterns sit on a sterile mathematical grid.

polyrhythm.definition: Two or more rhythmic streams with different subdivisions playing simultaneously. The streams realign every N beats (the LCM of their cycles).

3_against_2.definition: 3 evenly-spaced hits in the time of 2 beats (or 6 against 4). Most common polyrhythm.
3_against_2.feel: loose, flowing, slight tension between the two streams
best_for: any moment that wants to break the grid without breaking the meter
3_against_2.concrete_recipe: if 4/4 bar = 4 beats, place hits at 0, 0.6667, 1.3333, 2.0, 2.6667, 3.3333 (6 hits across 4 beats)

4_against_3.definition: 4 evenly-spaced hits in the time of 3 beats (used over 3/4 or as a hemiola in 4/4)
4_against_3.feel: very destabilizing — the 4 stream feels 'wrong' against 3
best_for: bridge moments that want metric ambiguity
intro polyrhythm to disorient before locking in
4_against_3.concrete_recipe: in 4/4 bar, place 4 evenly across 3 beats: 0, 0.75, 1.5, 2.25 (then beat 3 onwards is a different cycle)
4_against_3.warning: use sparingly — extended use sounds like a meter mistake

5_against_4.definition: 5 hits across 4 beats. Used in Bramure arp; realigns every 5 bars.
5_against_4.feel: shifting groove that drifts in and out of phase with the downbeat
best_for: moments that want continuous motion without locking
5_against_4.concrete_recipe: 5-note cell in 16ths cycles every 1.25 beats; over 4 bars (16 beats) the cell repeats 12.8 times — full realignment every 5 bars

7_against_4.feel: almost arrhythmic, requires anchoring against straight 4/4 to be perceived as polyrhythm not chaos
7_against_4.warning: Yorke would use this; Cobain never. Match to genre_blend before applying.

polyrhythm.anchoring_rule: When using polyrhythm, ALWAYS keep the kick/snare on straight 4/4 grid. The polyrhythm sits OVER the grid — never replace the grid. Without an anchor, polyrhythm becomes noise.

swing_ratios.definition: How a pair of 8th-notes is timed: the offbeat 8th can be exactly halfway (straight) or pushed later (swing).

light_swing.feel: subtle human laid-back, barely perceptible push
best_for: any pattern that should sound 'slightly drunk'

shuffle.warning: Shuffle is rare in industrial/grunge/electronic — apply only if explicitly intended

decision_tree_swing.if_genre_blend_yorke_pct >= 25: prefer light_swing for verse, straight for build/drop
decision_tree_swing.if_section == 'drop' OR section == 'fina: force straight — drops need precision for cluster impact

groove_feel.definition: Where hits land RELATIVE to the metronome. Same notation, different feel.

do_not_apply_to: sub bass — these MUST stay on grid for low-end clarity

behind_beat.feel: laid-back, drag, brooding, heavy — 'sitting in the pocket'

groove_feel.groove_feel_callback: Within a section, varying the groove_feel is a WOW lever — e.g. Verse drags behind-beat, Pre-Drop snaps to on-beat (the sudden tightness telegraphs the drop)

tempo_perception_at_95_bpm.comment: Tempo and feel interact. At 95 BPM (Venin / our preferred slow burn), 8th notes feel 'comfortable' — neither fast nor slow. 16ths feel 'busy'. Triplet 8ths (3-against-2) feel 'loose'.
rules: At 95 BPM, drum hi-hat 16ths sound aggressive (mechanical) — use sparingly
At 95 BPM, drum hi-hat 8ths feel restrained — best default
Triplet feels (6/8 or polyrhythm 3:2) at 95 BPM gravitate toward AIC / Yorke ballad territory
Cluster stabs at 95 BPM hit harder than at 120 — the slow tempo amplifies impact

rhythm_advanced.purpose: Advanced rhythmic devices that ELEVATE a pattern from competent to memorable. Each device is scored by wow_factor and explicit usage rules — overuse breaks the device's effect.

syncopation_typology.definition: Placing accents on weak beats or off-beats, displacing the listener's sense of where the downbeat is.

anticipation_push.definition: An accented hit lands 1/8 or 1/16 EARLIER than expected (push the beat). Listener's expectation of the downbeat creates impact.
anticipation_push.example_at_4_4: snare expected on beat 3 (=2.0) lands at 1.75 instead — 1/16 push
anticipation_push.warning: If everything pushes, nothing pushes. Use on 1-2 specific hits per phrase.

suspension_pull.definition: Accented hit lands 1/8 or 1/16 LATER than expected. Creates tension by withholding.
suspension_pull.example_at_4_4: snare expected on beat 3 lands at 2.25 instead — 1/16 drag

16th_displacement.definition: An entire phrase shifted by 1/16 from its expected grid position. Whole pattern feels off-kilter while remaining metric.
16th_displacement.example_at_4_4: kick pattern that should be [0, 1, 2, 3] becomes [0.25, 1.25, 2.25, 3.25] — same shape, displaced
16th_displacement.warning: Apply to ONE bar then resolve to grid — extended displacement loses meter

and_of_2_push.definition: Cobain signature: snare/vocal accent on the '&' of beat 2 (1.5) instead of the strict beat 2

tresillo_3_3_2.definition: 8th-note pattern grouped 3-3-2 within 4 beats (positions 0, 0.75, 1.5 in 8ths = 3+3+2). The Latin/dance hall building block.
tresillo_3_3_2.example_at_4_4: kick on positions 0, 1.5, 3.0 (8th-note grid steps 0, 3, 6 of 8)
tresillo_3_3_2.feel: natural pulse felt around the world
best_for: any kick pattern that wants forward momentum without 4-on-the-floor

hemiola.definition: Short-duration polyrhythm: 3 groups of 2 against 2 groups of 3 (or vice-versa) within a small span (typically 2-4 bars).

examples.3_groups_of_2_in_3_bars_4_4: in 12 beats, group hits as [4 4 4] (whole notes) instead of [3 3 3 3] — feels like the bar shifted to 3/4 temporarily
examples.2_groups_of_3_in_2_bars_3_4: in 6 beats, group hits as [3 3] instead of [2 2 2]

hemiola.feel: metric ambiguity that resolves — listener almost forgets the meter, then it snaps back
best_for: moments that want to break the grid temporarily
hemiola.warning: Hemiola MUST resolve back to the original meter — if it doesn't, listener loses the meter entirely

breakbeat.definition: Chopping a continuous drum pattern into discrete segments separated by silence or different patterns. Each chop creates a micro-section.

stutter.description: Repeat a tiny segment (1/16 to 1/4 beat) 2-4 times for glitch effect

drum_silence_drop.description: Cut ALL drums for 1-2 beats mid-pattern (often beats 3-4 of bar 4 of a phrase)

snare_only_bar.description: 1 bar where everything cuts except snare (which plays the standard 2/4 backbeat)

kick_only_bar.description: 1 bar where everything cuts except kick (continues the pulse)
best_for: after a section_silence — kick re-establishes the pulse

stop_time.definition: Cut the rhythm section (drums + bass) entirely while keeping melody/harmony moving. Inverts the listener's expectation of who carries pulse.
best_for: bridge moment that wants exposed melody
stop_time.comment: Stop-time is the rhythmic equivalent of false_relation — momentary suspension that resolves
stop_time.callback_strategy: stop_time at end of Verse 1 → callback at start of Final Drop with same melody but FULL rhythm section returns = payoff

subdivision_shifts.definition: Within a section, switching the dominant rhythmic subdivision (8ths -> 16ths -> triplets) — changes the perceived speed without changing tempo

8ths_to_16ths.where: transition from Verse to Pre-Drop (gives sense of acceleration)

16ths_to_triplets.where: Bridge — momentary loose feel before snapping back to straight 16ths in Final Drop

8ths_to_triplets_to_8ths.where: single bar of triplets sandwiched between 8th-note phrases — micro-disorientation

subdivision_halving.description: Suddenly halve the subdivision rate — 16ths drop to 8ths, instant breath

callback_through_rhythm.principle: A specific rhythmic motif (e.g., the and_of_2_push, or a 3-3-2 tresillo) that appears once in an early section CAN be restated in Final Drop for callback effect — even more powerful than a melodic callback because rhythm is felt physically.
examples: Verse 1 has and_of_2_push on snare → Final Drop bar 5 reuses and_of_2_push verbatim → physical recognition
Bridge introduces a 3:2 hemiola → Final Drop closing 2 bars do the same hemiola → 'home stretch' feel

density_curves.purpose: Notes-per-second and simultaneous-voice budgets per section type. The advisor uses these to ENFORCE the 'one idea per section' principle and to calibrate density-as-tension lever (gradual density increase = anticipation; sudden density inversion = WOW).
density_curves.principle_density_vs_intensity: Density (notes/sec) and intensity (perceived energy) are correlated but NOT identical. A Verse can be DENSE but LOW-energy (busy hi-hat + soft chord). A Drop can be SPARSE but HIGH-intensity (single huge cluster stab + silence). Density is a tool, intensity is the goal.

notes_per_second_targets_at_95_bpm.comment: These are SECTION-LEVEL averages, not bar-level. At 95 BPM, 1 beat = 0.632s. A 4/4 bar = 2.53s. An 8th-note pattern = ~3.16 hits/sec.

intro_atmospheric.anti_pattern: more than 6 voices = not an intro anymore

verse_groove.feel: established pulse, room for vocal/lead to breathe

pre_drop_buildup.feel: density INCREASING toward drop entry — riser + drum stutter
pre_drop_buildup.rule: the LAST bar of pre-drop should hit max nps for the section, then DROP to silence or single hit

drop_main.feel: cluster stabs SHARED across 6+ tracks — this counts as ONE rhythmic event, not 6
drop_main.key_insight: Drop nps lower than Pre-Drop because of NEGATIVE SPACE between cluster stabs — the silence between hits is the design

hollow_void.rule: must be UNDER intro nps — otherwise not a true hollow

bridge_modal.rule: lower density than Verse 2 (Bridge SHOULD feel less busy than what came before)

final_drop_fire.feel: highest density of the song BUT every voice has its lane (frequency_surrender enforced)
final_drop_fire.key_insight: Final Drop nps higher than Drop because density itself is part of the climax — but cluster_density of stabs is also higher (8 hits/bar vs 4-5)

outro_decay.feel: thinning, fading, single sustained voice carries last note
outro_decay.rule: must end UNDER intro nps — outro is a mirror of intro but LESS

polyphony_budget.purpose: Hard cap on simultaneous voices per track type — enforces frequency_surrender by reducing collision possibilities.

kick.comment: kick is monophonic — never 2 kicks at same time except cluster sync

snare.comment: monophonic — claps/snares stacked count as 1 event via cluster

drone.comment: single sustained note is the spine

lead_synth.comment: monophonic with octave double for emphasis

vocal.comment: monophonic vocal line — harmonies count as separate vocal_layer track

fx_riser.comment: single sustained pitch, timbre via automation

section_total_polyphony_max.comment: Sum across all active tracks. Exceeding = freq_collision_warning fired by advisor.

density_as_tension_lever.purpose: Density is a PROGRESSIVE lever — the most reliable way to build anticipation without adding new musical content.

gradual_increase.description: Across a 4-bar Pre-Drop, nps grows from 4 to 12. Each bar adds ~2 nps.
gradual_increase.example: Pre-Drop bar 1: kick + sub (4 nps), bar 2: + snare (6 nps), bar 3: + hihat 16ths (10 nps), bar 4: + roll/stutter (14 nps), then Drop entry RESETS to 8 nps with negative space

density_inversion_drop.description: After Pre-Drop max density, Drop entry is SPARSER not denser. The contrast hits harder.
density_inversion_drop.example: Pre-Drop bar 4 = 14 nps stutter, Drop bar 1 = 4 huge cluster stabs (3-4 nps but each stab is 6 tracks aligned)
density_inversion_drop.comment: Counter-intuitive: density DECREASE at the drop = more impact than density INCREASE

negative_space_within_density.description: In a high-density section, plant 1 bar of low-density (subbar_silence on hihat) — listener hears the breath
negative_space_within_density.example: Final Drop bar 13: hihat mutes for 1 bar while everything else continues = listener feels the gap subliminally

callback_through_density.principle: A specific density profile in an early section can be RESTATED later for callback effect.
callback_through_density.example: Verse 1 has a unique density signature (e.g., kick + tom_low + sus pad = 5 nps). Bridge uses this exact density signature mid-section = listener subliminally recognizes 'we were here before'.

anti_patterns_density: Drop denser than Final Drop (Final Drop must be PEAK density)
Hollow denser than Intro (Hollow MUST be the lowest density of the song)
Verse 2 less dense than Verse 1 (when V2 should ADD complexity to V1)
Continuous density from Pre-Drop into Drop (the contrast IS the impact)
All sections at similar density (no shape, no journey)

_TBD_D3: genres (Cobain, Reznor, Yorke, Daft Punk) + per-genre conditional rules

default_personality_blend._note: Sum = 100. More Daft Punk than Yorke per user preference. Each genre's >=25% threshold triggers its conditional rules (D3). NOTE: each axis represents a CLUSTER of related artists (see reference_albums below) — 'cobain_pct' is shorthand for grunge/alt-rock 90s influence as a whole, not just Nirvana.

_meta.intent: ELECTRONIC MUSIC PRODUCTION ADVISOR. The 35-song dissection (Nirvana, Soundgarden, Pumpkins, NIN, QOTSA, Daft Punk, Radiohead) is RESEARCH MATERIAL — the principles documented there inform compositions in techno, deep house, ambient, IDM, glitch, downtempo, trip-hop, synthwave, dark-electronic, EBM, dub-techno, neo-classical-electronic, electronic-pop, etc. The advisor's OUTPUT is recipes ACTIONABLE in a Live/Logic/Pro-Tools session targeting electronic-music projects, NOT a rock-band-emulation toolkit.
_meta.purpose: Operational index extracting all advisor recipes from song_dissection_exhaustive into a queryable network. Transforms 35-song research artifact into actionable composition tool for ELECTRONIC MUSIC PROJECTS. Each recipe enriched with multi-dimensional metadata + electronic-application translation; cross-referenced via synergy/conflict/evolution edges; projected through 7 lenses; pre-computed for default personality blend (Cobain 30 / Reznor 30 / Daft Punk 25 / Yorke 15 — already 55%-electronic-leaning); clustered into composition path templates targeting specific electronic sub-genres.
_meta.derivation: Generated by reading every advisor_extract block in song_dissection_exhaustive.by_artist and lifting each core_compositional_recipes entry into a recipe atom. Source song / album / artist / production_philosophy_lineage preserved as provenance. Wow_factor + personality_fit copied verbatim. Composition_stage / compositional_axis / genre_vocabulary / applicability_tags / personality_dominance derived from rule + applicability text. Synergy / conflict / evolution edges deduced from co-presence in songs + from explicit comparisons in dissection text. ELECTRONIC_TRANSLATION + ELECTRONIC_TARGET_GENRES added per atom in B-phase backfill, reframing each rock-source principle as actionable electronic-music recipe.
design_principles: Atom enriched once, indexed many ways — no recipe duplication; lenses are pointer collections.
Synergies are EVIDENCE-BACKED, not invented: two recipes link only if they co-appear in a documented song's advisor_extract.
Conflicts are LOGICAL, not aesthetic: pairs flagged only when one recipe's rule contradicts another's premise (e.g., extreme_compressed_economy vs. extended_form).
Personality dominance is COMPUTED, not assigned: max(personality_fit_breakdown) wins; ties → 'balanced'.
Wow_factor tiers are FIXED bands, not curve-fit percentiles: top ≥0.85, gold 0.80-0.84, silver 0.75-0.79, bronze 0.70-0.74, supporting <0.70.

Composition path templates are COHERENT BUNDLES, not arbitrary selections: each template is a self-contained recipe set spanning composition→mastering stages, targeting a specific electronic sub-genre.

ELECTRONIC TRANSLATION is the bridge: each atom carries both the rock-source rule (provenance, fidelity to documented examples) AND the electronic-application reformulation (actionable in DAW + electronic-music sub-genre context). Without translation, the corpus is a rock encyclopedia; WITH translation, it's an electronic-music composer's tool.
intended_consumers: human electronic-music composer using advisor manually (browse by lens, filter by electronic_target_genres, pick from default_blend_projection top-30, follow a composition_path_template)
future Python wrapper exposing recipes via programmatic API
Mix Analyzer integration (recipes inform EQ8 automation suggestions per track section in Ableton Live electronic-music projects)

composition_stage.description: Production-pipeline stage where the recipe applies. Recipes from a song's composition layer go here, recipes from arrangement layer there, etc. Maps directly to 8-layer schema used in song_dissection_exhaustive.

values.composition: Pre-arrangement decisions: harmonic vocabulary, meter, tempo, structure, melodic intent. Independent of instrumentation.
values.arrangement: Instrumentation + density + sectional structure + element deployment across the song's arc.
values.performance: How musicians play: feel, dynamics, technique, vocal delivery, drumming philosophy.
values.recording: Tracking decisions: mic placement, room choice, gear selection, take-comping philosophy, producer-engineer role configuration.
values.mixing: Post-tracking decisions: balance, panning, EQ, compression, automation, reverb, spatial treatment.
values.mastering: Final-stage decisions: loudness target, dynamic preservation, spectral balance, format-specific delivery.
values.philosophy: Cross-stage production-philosophy decisions (auteur model, unified-role, band-democracy, etc.) that frame all other stages.

compositional_axis.description: Which musical dimension the recipe primarily addresses. A recipe may belong to multiple axes; primary axis listed first.

values.rhythm: Meter, tempo, groove, asymmetry, polyrhythm, swing, programmed vs. live, drum-feature, ambiguous-vs-clear-meter.
values.harmony: Chord progression, voice-leading, modal vocabulary, drone, suspension, classical-influence, filter-modulation-as-harmonic-motion.
values.melody: Vocal phrasing, melodic contour, hook design, falsetto vs. chest-voice, vocoded-vocal-as-instrument.
values.structure: Form, section economy, dynamic arc, build-and-release, extended-form, compressed-economy, multi-section, late-entry, hard-soft-hard.
values.texture: Layering, density, processing, sound design, sample manipulation, distortion deployment, atmospheric pads, industrial textures.
values.dynamics: Volume relationships, quiet-loud contrast, dynamic-arc preservation, restraint, explosion, mix automation, mastering loudness choices.
values.voice: Vocal identity, delivery character, vocal democracy, scream-to-sing, processed-vocal, harmony contributions, vocal-as-instrument.

genre_vocabulary.description: Genre-tradition the recipe draws from OR targets. SOURCE values describe where the principle was documented (rock-side, classical, jazz). TARGET values describe electronic-music sub-genres where the translated recipe applies. The field is bidirectional — used both to filter by stylistic provenance and to filter by intended electronic-music output context.

values._source_values_section: --- SOURCE GENRES (where the principles were documented) ---
values.rock_traditional: Standard rock-band format (guitar + bass + drums + vocal), verse-chorus structure, 4/4 priority.
values.punk_hardcore: Fast tempo, aggressive vocal, compressed economy, raw texture.
values.stoner_desert_rock: Drop tunings, hypnotic riff repetition, groove-priority, Sabbath/Kyuss lineage.
values.industrial: Programmed drums, processed textures, distorted bass synth, samplers, aggressive production.
values.electronic_dance: 4-on-the-floor, filter modulation, club-system orientation, programmed foundation, sustained tempo.
values.art_rock: Genre-flexible compositional ambition, classical/jazz influences, intentional ambiguity, experimental tracking decisions.
values.classical_influenced: Voice-leading craft, chromatic harmony, orchestral instrumentation, classical-tradition compositional templates.
values.free_jazz_influenced: Locked rhythm + free expression, live brass/horns, controlled cacophony, Mingus lineage.
values.filter_house: French house tradition, vocoded vocal phrases, analog synth + filter sweep as compositional engine.
values.modular_electronic: Modular synth patterns, evolving textures via filter+envelope modulation, film-scoring discipline integration.
values.hybrid_industrial_rock: Industrial production vocabulary deployed in rock-anthem structure (NIN We're In This Together, Wretched).
values.hybrid_classical_electronic: Classical harmonic progression in electronic context (Daft Punk Veridis Quo, Radiohead Pyramid Song).
values.hybrid_genre_clash: Two opposed genre vocabularies co-existing within single song (Radiohead National Anthem rock + free-jazz-brass).
values._target_values_section: --- TARGET ELECTRONIC GENRES (where translated recipes apply, used in electronic_target_genres field) ---
values.techno: Driving 4/4 at 125-140 BPM, machine-precise programmed drums, repetitive minimalism with subtle evolution, club-system orientation. Detroit/Berlin lineage. Sub-genres: minimal, peak-time, melodic, industrial.
values.deep_house: 4/4 at 118-126 BPM, soulful chord stabs, atmospheric pads, vocal samples, warmer than techno. Larry Heard / Moodymann lineage.
values.ambient: No fixed tempo or 60-90 BPM, sustained synth textures, evolving harmonic fields, no percussion or sparse atmospheric percussion, listener-meditative. Eno / Aphex Twin SAW II lineage.
values.IDM: Intelligent Dance Music — complex programmed rhythms, irregular meters, glitch-tolerant, melodic-experimental. Aphex Twin / Autechre / Boards of Canada lineage.
values.glitch: Foregrounded digital artifacts (clicks, cuts, micro-edits, buffer-corruption sounds) as compositional material. Oval / Pole / clicks-and-cuts lineage.
values.downtempo: 60-100 BPM, jazz/dub/hip-hop influences, lush production, listener-attentive rather than dance-functional. Massive Attack / Portishead / DJ Shadow lineage.
values.trip_hop: Slow head-nod tempo, sample-based, melancholic, often vocal-led with atmospheric production. Bristol scene / Massive Attack / Portishead lineage.
values.synthwave: 80s-revival aesthetic, gated reverbs, FM-synth leads, vintage drum machines, retrofuturism. Kavinsky / Carpenter Brut lineage.
values.dark_electronic: Reznor-adjacent industrial-influenced electronic, distorted bass, sample-based aggression, often non-4/4. NIN / Trent Reznor solo / How to Destroy Angels lineage.
values.EBM: Electronic Body Music — driving 4/4 with industrial rhythm-emphasis, distorted vocals, cold synthesizers. Front 242 / Nitzer Ebb lineage.
values.dub_techno: Techno's 4/4 framework + dub-reggae's spatial production (deep delays, long reverbs, filter-modulated chord stabs). Basic Channel / Maurizio / Rhythm and Sound lineage.
values.neo_classical_electronic: Classical instrumentation + electronic processing, modern composition with electronic toolkit. Nils Frahm / Max Richter / Jóhann Jóhannsson lineage.
values.electronic_pop: Pop-song structure (3-4 min verse-chorus) with electronic production vocabulary. Daft Punk Discovery / Justice / electronic crossover.

applicability_tags.description: Fine-grained semantic tags that describe specific compositional/production techniques. A recipe carries multiple tags; tags enable cross-recipe pattern matching beyond the broader axes.

values.extreme_tempo: Tempos above ~200 BPM or below ~70 BPM — outside typical rock-pop range.
values.compressed_economy: Track runtime < 3 minutes — compositional density via brevity.
values.extended_form: Track runtime > 6 minutes — compositional ambition via length.
values.drone_foundation: Sustained low-frequency drone as harmonic anchor.
values.modal_voicings: Suspended/quartal/quintal voicings replacing traditional chord progression.
values.hypnotic_repetition: Riff/pattern repeated near-identically with micro-variations for hypnotic groove.
values.dynamic_arc_explosive: Patient build to massive cathartic explosion (Soma-style).
values.dynamic_arc_subtle: Patient build to gentle emotional release (TDTWWA-style).
values.hard_soft_hard_contrast: Aggressive verse → melodic chorus → aggressive verse within single song.
values.wall_of_sound_layering: Many guitar/synth layers EQ-staggered + panned for dense harmonic field.
values.minimum_element_count: Few elements, each earning maximum compositional weight.
values.drum_feature_section: Compositionally-integrated drum spotlight (A Song for the Dead-style).
values.late_drum_entry: Drums absent at song-start, enter mid-song for perceptual shift (Pyramid Song-style).
values.drumless_arrangement: No drums at all — rhythm conveyed via piano/bass/samples (EIRP-style).
values.ambiguous_meter: Rhythm intentionally hard to count — felt rather than measured.
values.filter_modulation_as_motion: Analog filter sweep substitutes for traditional harmonic motion (Around the World-style).
values.vocoded_vocal_as_instrument: Vocoder applied to vocal making it textural rather than narrative.
values.pitch_shifted_vocal: Pitch-shifting processing applied as compositional element.
values.scream_to_sing_contrast: Single vocalist deploys aggressive scream + melodic sing within one song.
values.vocal_democracy: Multiple band members lead vocals on different tracks within an album.
values.guest_vocal_harmony: Documented guest vocalist contributes harmony or lead (Lanegan-style).
values.documentary_spoken_word: Interview/narration audio integrated as compositional element.
values.live_band_in_electronic_context: Session musicians playing in electronic-music production paradigm.
values.programmed_drums_as_composition: Drum machine programming as compositional instrument, not stand-in for live drums.
values.live_drums_with_programmed_overlay: Live drummer with sample triggers + electronic percussion blended.
values.sample_based_foundation: Composition built on cleared sample loop from prior recording.
values.manipulated_vocal_samples: Vocal sampled then granular/pitch/time-processed as instrumental texture.
values.metallic_percussion_loop: Sample-based metallic/found-sound percussion as rhythmic foundation.
values.ondes_martenot_or_rare_classical: Rare classical-orchestral instrument used for spectral color.
values.free_jazz_brass: Brass section playing free-jazz expressionism over locked rhythm.
values.extensive_mix_automation: Section-specific automation rides drum bus, guitar layers, vocals, reverbs.
values.dynamic_preservation_mastering: Mastering deliberately less compressed than commercial standard.
values.auteur_recording_environment: Non-commercial recording space with documented atmospheric character.
values.analog_tape_tracking: Recording to analog tape rather than digital DAW capture.
values.deliberate_genre_reinvention: Album-level genre reinvention from prior catalog identity.
values.anchor_track_within_reinvention: Former-genre track retained within reinvention album for listener anchor.
values.production_partnership_evolution: Auteur-producer expands partnership across multi-album catalog.
values.classical_influenced_voice_leading: Multi-voice tracking with classical-tradition voice-leading craft.
values.groove_over_complexity: Pocket + feel prioritized over technical demonstration.
values.atmospheric_continuity: Sustained atmosphere as primary aesthetic, not quiet/loud contrast.
values.rhythmic_emphasis_over_harmony: Rhythm as primary compositional axis, harmonic stasis acceptable.

personality_dominance.description: Computed: max(personality_fit_breakdown) wins. If two are within 5 points → 'balanced'. Used to filter recipes by personality lean. The four personalities (Cobain, Reznor, Daft Punk, Yorke) are taken from the user's documented advisor framework.

values.cobain_dominant: Punk/grunge sensibility: raw, unpolished, emotionally direct, riff-driven, capture-philosophy.
values.reznor_dominant: Industrial/auteur sensibility: programmed, textural, scream-to-sing, single-creator-driven.
values.daft_punk_dominant: Electronic/dance sensibility: programmed-foundation, filter-modulation, hypnotic-cyclic, duo-auteur.
values.yorke_dominant: Art-rock/intentional-ambiguity sensibility: classical-influenced, atmospheric, falsetto, compositional uncertainty.
values.balanced: No single personality > 5 points above the second-highest. Recipe spans multiple sensibilities equally.

wow_factor_tiers.description: Fixed bands for wow_factor scoring. Each recipe pre-tagged with its tier for quick filtering. Bands chosen to roughly correspond to: top = once-in-a-decade compositional choices; gold = highly ambitious; silver = strong; bronze = solid; supporting = useful but not standalone-impressive.

values.top: wow_factor >= 0.85 — exceptional compositional ambition, the rarest payoffs.
values.gold: wow_factor 0.80 to 0.849 — highly ambitious, well-documented impact.
values.bronze: wow_factor 0.70 to 0.749 — solid, supporting role within larger recipe set.
values.supporting: wow_factor < 0.70 — useful in combination, rarely standalone.

production_philosophy_lineage.description: Which of the 12 documented production philosophies the recipe derives from. Each philosophy has its own aesthetic + workflow signature; tagging recipes by lineage enables filtering for production-philosophy coherence in composition path templates.

values.albini_capture: Pure capture, room-prominent, minimal shaping (Nirvana In Utero 1993).
values.vig_wallace_layered_wide: Meticulous tracking + width-maximized mixing (Nevermind 1991, Siamese Dream 1993).
values.kasper_unified_band_driven: Unified engineer/producer/mixer + band-driven (Soundgarden Down on the Upside 1996).
values.flood_moulder_corgan_three_way: Textural-experimental three-way producer credit (Pumpkins Mellon Collie 1995).
values.reznor_solo_auteur: Solo auteur-producer + programmed-sampled industrial (NIN Spiral 1994 → Hesitation Marks 2013).
values.valentine_unified_groove_rock: Engineer-producer-mixer unified for power-trio groove rock (QOTSA Songs for the Deaf 2002).
values.barresi_unified_heavy_rock: Engineer-producer-mixer unified with heavy-rock specialization (QOTSA Lullabies 2005).
values.daft_punk_duo_auteur_home_studio: Duo auteur fully self-produced in home studio (Homework 1997 + Discovery 2001).
values.daft_punk_ram_massively_expanded: Duo auteur + live session musicians + analog studios + external mixer (RAM 2013).
values.godrich_unified_sixth_member: Engineer-producer-mixer unified with band-creative-partnership (Radiohead OK Computer 1997 → Amnesiac 2001).

recipe_atom_schema._description: Reference schema for each recipe atom inside `recipes`. Documented here for human readers + future tooling; not duplicated per-atom.
recipe_atom_schema._intent_clarification: The 35-song dissection is RESEARCH MATERIAL drawn from rock/electronic catalogs. The advisor's PURPOSE is to inform ELECTRONIC MUSIC PRODUCTION (techno, deep house, ambient, IDM, glitch, downtempo, synthwave, dark electronic, EBM, neo-classical-electronic, etc.) — not to reproduce rock songs. Each atom carries TWO layers: (1) the rock-source provenance (rule, documented_example, source) preserves where the principle was learned, (2) the electronic-application layer (electronic_translation, electronic_target_genres) reframes the principle as a concrete recipe for an electronic-music composer.

fields.id: snake_case identifier, globally unique within recipes_index. Inherited from advisor_extract.core_compositional_recipes[].id.
fields.rule: The rule text — single sentence describing what the recipe prescribes IN ITS SOURCE CONTEXT. Inherited verbatim from dissection. Preserves the rock/electronic-source language for fidelity.
fields.wow_factor: Float 0.0-1.0. Inherited verbatim from source recipe.
fields.source: Object: {artist, song, album, year}. Provenance preserved.
fields.compositional_axis: Array of 1-3 values from compositional_axis axis (primary first).
fields.genre_vocabulary: Array of 1-2 values from genre_vocabulary axis. SOURCE genres (where the principle was documented).
fields.applicability_tags: Array of 2-5 values from applicability_tags axis.
fields.personality_dominance: Computed value from personality_dominance axis (argmax + tie-break).
fields.personality_fit_breakdown: Object: {cobain, reznor, daft_punk, yorke} with integer percentages summing to 100.
fields.trade_offs: String — inherited verbatim. Costs / risks / tradeoffs of using this recipe.
fields.documented_example: String — inherited verbatim. Source song that exemplifies the recipe (rock or electronic).
fields.electronic_translation: STRING — concrete reformulation of the rule for an electronic-music composer. Strips rock-band-specific language (guitars, drum kit, room mics) and substitutes electronic equivalents (synth pads, programmed drums, modular patches, vocoder, sample manipulation, filter modulation, DAW-internal processing). Names specific electronic-music techniques + sub-genre application contexts. Goal: make the recipe IMMEDIATELY ACTIONABLE in a Live/Logic/Pro-Tools session targeting techno/deep-house/ambient/IDM/glitch/downtempo/etc.
fields.electronic_target_genres: Array of 1-3 values from genre_vocabulary axis (TARGET subset). Indicates which electronic sub-genres the translated recipe particularly fits. Values: techno, deep_house, ambient, IDM, glitch, downtempo, trip_hop, synthwave, dark_electronic, EBM, dub_techno, neo_classical_electronic, electronic_pop, filter_house, modular_electronic.
fields.synergy_recipes: Array of recipe ids that COMBINE WELL with this one (evidence: co-presence in same song's advisor_extract).
fields.conflict_recipes: Array of recipe ids that CONTRADICT this one (logical conflict in rule premises).
fields.evolution_recipes: Array of recipe ids that represent VARIATIONS of the same idea at different scales / contexts.

recipes._note: Populated incrementally in steps A2-A8. Each recipe atom keyed by its id, value is full atom per recipe_atom_schema. Provenance preserved via source field.

microtonal_bend_as_signature_riff.rule: A central instrumental hook can be made unforgettable by a deliberate MICROTONAL element (string bend that lands slightly out of pitch, or a synth detune of ~10 cents on key notes). The microtonal element ANCHORS the riff's identity — listeners associate the song with the slightly-off pitch.
microtonal_bend_as_signature_riff.electronic_translation: On your signature synth lead/arp/motif, automate PITCH (not vibrato) to drift ~10-25 cents sharp or flat on KEY NOTES of the phrase. In Live: pitch-bend automation on a MIDI clip, OR Pitch effect with manual ±cents drift, OR Operator's per-oscillator detune. In hardware: Sub 37 / Prophet pitch-wheel automation. The drift should be DELIBERATE (always on the same note, always the same amount) — not random. Other techniques: Just-Intonation tuning on a single Operator oscillator (different from 12-TET equal temperament), or microtonal scales via Scaler 2 / MTS-ESP. Apply for: IDM melodic motifs (Aphex-style), neo-classical-electronic harmonic richness, ambient drone-detune, glitch synth-quirk character. The microtonal anchor makes the listener's ear catch on the 'wrong' note as the song's identity.
microtonal_bend_as_signature_riff.applicability_text: Signature guitar/synth riffs; any single line that needs immediate identification; intros that establish identity in 4 bars.
microtonal_bend_as_signature_riff.trade_offs: If overused (every line is microtonal), the effect becomes random rather than deliberate. Use ONCE per song as the signature element.
microtonal_bend_as_signature_riff.documented_example: Heart-Shaped Box opening guitar riff — Cobain's bent string lands slightly sharp; the song's identity hinges on it.

slow_burn_arrival_not_explosion.rule: Build from intro through verse 1 / chorus 1 / verse 2 / chorus 2 / bridge / outro WITHOUT any single section being a detonation. The song's true peak is the OUTRO — sustained intensity arrived at gradually, not via an explosive chorus drop. Maturity over youthful fireworks.
slow_burn_arrival_not_explosion.electronic_translation: Compose 4-7 min track where NO drop / explosion / peak-density chorus happens — instead, intensity climbs IMPERCEPTIBLY across the full runtime, arriving at the song's emotional peak in the OUTRO, not the chorus. Each section adds ~5-10% density vs. previous, never +50%. Choruses don't drop kick — they thicken pads. Outro accumulates everything plus reverb expansion. Apply for: ambient-techno (Burial, Andy Stott), dub-techno (Basic Channel — 8min tracks with sub-perceptible build), downtempo (Bonobo deep cuts), neo-classical-electronic (Nils Frahm slow climbs). Avoid in dance-floor-functional techno or EBM (those need DROP). The slow-burn aesthetic rewards attentive listening; won't work in playlist-skim contexts.
slow_burn_arrival_not_explosion.applicability_text: Ballad-format dark songs; songs that want mature weight rather than youthful explosion; tracks 4+ minutes long that need internal pacing.
slow_burn_arrival_not_explosion.trade_offs: Demands listener patience + attention. Won't work in radio-single contexts. Risk of feeling anticlimactic if outro doesn't justify the build.
slow_burn_arrival_not_explosion.documented_example: Heart-Shaped Box — 4:40 of patient build to a sustained outro rather than a Smells-Like-Teen-Spirit-style chorus detonation.

vocal_layer_count_as_dynamic_lever.rule: Verse: SINGLE vocal. Chorus: DOUBLED vocal. Outro: STACKED multiple vocals. The perceived 'size' of each section is layer count, not just volume. Listener responds to density change without needing dynamic-arc explosion.
vocal_layer_count_as_dynamic_lever.electronic_translation: Section-by-section vocal layer count — arrangement-view automation on vocal-stack tracks. Verse: dry mono vocal centered. Pre-chorus: add doubled-vocal slightly detuned (5-15 cents) panned soft L+R. Chorus: stack 3-5 vocals — original + harmony at 5th + octave below + vocoder layer + reverb-only ghost. Bridge/outro: full 7-12 vocal stack including pitched-down formant + vocoded harmony cluster + granular vocal-shimmer above. Use Live's track grouping + automation per-track. Process each layer differently (one saturated, one cleaned, one filtered, one pitch-shifted) so the stack reads as TEXTURE, not as identical vocals. Apply for: electronic-pop (Imogen Heap / Bon Iver-electronic style), trip-hop (Massive Attack 'Teardrop' lineage), downtempo emotional climaxes, dark-electronic chorus density. Daft Punk Discovery vocoded-stack tradition.
vocal_layer_count_as_dynamic_lever.applicability_text: Verse-Chorus dynamics that should feel 'bigger' without louder mastering; any section transition wanting density shift.
vocal_layer_count_as_dynamic_lever.trade_offs: Requires multi-take vocal capacity + careful comping. Risk of layered chorus sounding artificial if takes too tightly aligned.
vocal_layer_count_as_dynamic_lever.documented_example: Heart-Shaped Box vocal architecture: single verse → doubled chorus → stacked outro driving the song's slow burn.

feedback_as_compositional_layer_in_bridg.rule: Bridge has SUSTAINED guitar feedback (or synth pad equivalent) underneath the lead instrument's solo. Feedback is treated as harmonic FOUNDATION, not transitional artifact — it sits in the mix as a compositional layer for the bridge's full duration.
feedback_as_compositional_layer_in_bridg.electronic_translation: In the bridge/breakdown section, place a SUSTAINED HIGH-RESONANCE FILTER FEEDBACK (resonance pushed near self-oscillation on a Moog ladder / Diva / Cytomic) or a TUNED FEEDBACK LOOP (output of a delay routed to its input through a high-pass) under the lead synth/vocal. The feedback sits at the song's tonic + 5th, sustains the entire bridge duration, builds slowly. Alternative: granular sustain with cloud/freezer (Output Portal, Granulator II) frozen on a single note. Or noise-bed pitched to tonic + filtered. Apply for: dark-electronic bridges (NIN-adjacent), IDM atmospheric breaks, ambient long-form sustain, dub-techno breakdown harmonic field. The feedback isn't an accident — it's the bridge's HARMONIC BED, replacing chord changes with sustained tension.
feedback_as_compositional_layer_in_bridg.applicability_text: Bridge moments needing 'broken' atmospheric texture; instrumental sections wanting underlying tension.
feedback_as_compositional_layer_in_bridg.trade_offs: Requires controlled-feedback technique or synth substitution. Risk of muddiness if feedback frequency clashes with bridge harmony.
feedback_as_compositional_layer_in_bridg.documented_example: Heart-Shaped Box bridge — sustained feedback under the lead line treated as harmonic bed.

albini_vs_litt_two_mix_versions.rule: When mixing, consider whether the song wants RAW LIVE-DOCUMENT character (Albini approach: room mics emphasized, no master comp, vocal sits in band) OR COMMERCIAL POLISH character (Litt approach: subtle EQ + reverb + vocal forward + light master compression). The same recording can be mixed two ways; choose per song's artistic intent rather than imposing one philosophy across the album.
albini_vs_litt_two_mix_versions.electronic_translation: Render TWO MIX VERSIONS of the same arrangement: (1) RAW capture-style: minimal master-bus processing (no master compressor / no sub-bass-EQ / no master limiter beyond peak-protection at -1 dBTP). Sounds at -14 LUFS integrated, dynamic, slightly under-loud vs. commercial. Best for ambient / dub-techno / downtempo / neo-classical-electronic releases targeting attentive listening / vinyl / Bandcamp. (2) COMMERCIAL polish: aggressive bus-compressor (Vari-Mu or SSL G), master-EQ tilt + presence boost, multi-band glue, soft-clip + limiter to -8 to -9 LUFS. Best for techno / EBM / dark-electronic / electronic-pop targeting club rotation / Spotify / streaming-loudness-normalization-aware playlists. Choose per track's artistic context. Some artists release BOTH versions (album-mix + club-mix). Apply meta-decision before mastering hand-off.
albini_vs_litt_two_mix_versions.applicability_text: Mix decisions on tracks where rawness vs polish is the artistic choice; alternative mix philosophy when commercial template doesn't serve the song.
albini_vs_litt_two_mix_versions.trade_offs: Demands honesty about song's artistic intent. Producing two mixes doubles studio time + budget. Risk of indecision if no clear aesthetic compass.
albini_vs_litt_two_mix_versions.documented_example: In Utero documented as having alternate mixes (Albini original vs. Scott Litt commercial-polish for radio singles like Heart-Shaped Box + All Apologies).

delayed_rhythm_section_entry_4_bars.rule: Open the song with the LEAD MELODIC INSTRUMENT alone for 4 bars (in HSB's case, guitar with the bend riff). Bass + drums delayed entry by 4 full bars. Establishes melodic identity before rhythmic foundation arrives — listener focuses on the riff before lock-in.
delayed_rhythm_section_entry_4_bars.electronic_translation: Open with LEAD synth motif / arpeggio / vocoded phrase alone for 4-8 bars (8-16 seconds at 120 BPM). Drums + bass DELAYED to bar 5 or bar 9. Listener locks onto melodic identity before lock-in. Variant for ambient: lead alone for 16-32 bars, kick never enters. Variant for techno: lead alone 4 bars, kick enters bar 5 with full pattern (classic Berghain opener pattern). Apply for: techno opener tracks, ambient track introductions, IDM melodic-foreground intros, downtempo gentle-arrival starts, electronic-pop hook-first openings. Solid arrangement-view discipline: NEVER lead all instruments enter on bar 1.
delayed_rhythm_section_entry_4_bars.applicability_text: Intros that center a melodic hook over groove; songs where the riff IS the song.
delayed_rhythm_section_entry_4_bars.trade_offs: Risk of intro feeling thin if melodic instrument lacks identity. 4-bar count is genre-specific; ballad may want 8 bars, punk 2 bars.
delayed_rhythm_section_entry_4_bars.documented_example: Heart-Shaped Box opens with guitar bend riff for 4 bars before bass + drums enter — riff identity established first.

cello_orchestral_pad_in_rock.rule: Add a sustained CELLO part (or bowed string equivalent — viola, violin section, even a synth pad emulating bowed strings) UNDERNEATH a rock band arrangement. Cello provides emotional weight + harmonic depth that distortion or volume cannot. Cello sits in low-mid register, complementing bass without competing.
cello_orchestral_pad_in_rock.electronic_translation: Use SAMPLED OR LIVE CELLO (Spitfire LABS Cellos, Sample Modeling Solo Strings, Cinematic Strings, OR record a real cellist if budget allows) sustained at the song's tonic + 5th in the LOW-MID register (~120-300 Hz fundamental, harmonics extending up). Sit it 3-6 dB BELOW your synth bass + 6 dB BELOW the lead synth. Light tape saturation gives it presence; no aggressive compression (preserves bow envelope). Apply for: neo-classical-electronic (essential — Nils Frahm / Ólafur Arnalds / Max Richter use this constantly), ambient harmonic deepening, downtempo emotional weight, dark-electronic dramatic verses, trip-hop atmospheric beds. The cello adds organic-warmth that pure synth can't deliver — an ANCHOR to acoustic-music tradition within an electronic track. For bigger weight: layer cello + viola + double-bass arco as a string trio. Stems available from many sample libraries.
cello_orchestral_pad_in_rock.applicability_text: Ballad tracks needing emotional weight without volume; verse arrangements wanting body without distortion; bridge sections departing from rock palette.
cello_orchestral_pad_in_rock.trade_offs: Requires available cellist or convincing string emulation. Risk of feeling stylistically forced if rock material doesn't support orchestral weight.
cello_orchestral_pad_in_rock.documented_example: Dumb's cello part by Kera Schaley — sustained underneath the rock band, providing emotional weight that distortion alone cannot deliver.

flat_dynamic_arc_anti_buildup.rule: Compose a song with NO dynamic peak. Verse and chorus at SAME intensity. No bridge build. No outro climax. The emotional impact comes from VOCAL DELIVERY + arrangement texture, not from architectural release. Anti-arc as artistic statement.
flat_dynamic_arc_anti_buildup.electronic_translation: Compose a 3-5 min track at constant density throughout — no drop, no buildup, no peak section. Same kick pattern + bass-line + pad-stack from bar 1 to last bar. Variation comes from MICRO-TEXTURAL changes (subtle filter shift on pad, occasional percussion accent, vocal-phrase variation) rather than density changes. Master to the SAME LUFS reading at every 30s window. This rejects the dance-music drop tradition entirely. Apply for: ambient (default mode — Eno, GAS, Stars of the Lid), drone-music, dub-techno (constant pulse with subtle variation), minimal-techno (arrangement-as-texture not arrangement-as-arc). Strong vocal performance OR strong textural identity required to sustain attention without architectural variation.
flat_dynamic_arc_anti_buildup.applicability_text: Slow burn tracks where dynamic shifts would feel cheap; songs prioritizing texture and vocal nuance over architecture; tracks following a more aggressive piece on an album (gives breathing room).
flat_dynamic_arc_anti_buildup.trade_offs: Requires VERY strong vocal melody and lyrical content to sustain listener interest without dynamic variation. Without these, the flat arc feels boring rather than meditative.
flat_dynamic_arc_anti_buildup.documented_example: Dumb maintains uniform intensity throughout — emotional impact from Cobain's vocal nuance + cello pad rather than dynamic build.

harmonic_minimalism_one_cycle_repeated.rule: Use the SAME 3-4 chord cycle for the ENTIRE song. No bridge changing chord, no chorus elevation, no key shift. The repetition creates a meditative, circular quality — listener inhabits the harmonic space rather than tracking progression. Vocal carries variation; harmony stays still.
harmonic_minimalism_one_cycle_repeated.electronic_translation: Set ONE 3-4 chord cycle (e.g., i — VI — III — VII in Aeolian, or i — iv — v — i in classical-minor) and loop it for the entire track 4-7 minutes. NEVER modulate, NEVER change chord choices. Variation comes from filter modulation on the chord stack, layer addition/subtraction, occasional pad swap, or melodic variation in lead synth ABOVE the static chords. Apply for: deep house (Larry Heard / Moodymann tradition — single chord progression across entire 7-min track), dub-techno (Maurizio aesthetic — same chord stab filtered + dubbed), ambient (Eno music-for-airports), neo-classical-electronic (Nils Frahm 'Says' single-progression). Demands strong melodic content OR strong textural identity to hold attention without harmonic motion.
harmonic_minimalism_one_cycle_repeated.applicability_text: Songs where vocal delivery is the centerpiece; tracks with strong textural identity (cello, distinctive guitar tone) where harmony would compete; ballad-format songs aiming for hypnotic restraint.
harmonic_minimalism_one_cycle_repeated.trade_offs: Requires strong melodic content + textural identity to sustain interest. Risk of feeling underwritten if vocal/texture not compelling.
harmonic_minimalism_one_cycle_repeated.documented_example: Dumb's same chord cycle throughout — Cobain's vocal carries the variation, harmony stays still as meditation.

compact_runtime_2_to_3_minutes.rule: Restrict song length to 2:30-3:00. Resist the urge to add a bridge or extended outro. Listener leaves the song wanting more — which is more powerful than full satisfaction. Compositional discipline via brevity.
compact_runtime_2_to_3_minutes.electronic_translation: Lock arrangement-view length at 2:30-3:00. NO breakdown, NO bridge, NO extended outro. Verse A → Chorus → Verse B → Chorus → ABRUPT END or short outro. Demands every section earn its place — no extension for length's sake. Apply for: electronic-pop singles (Justice / Daft Punk Discovery 'Aerodynamic' compact economy), interludes within longer albums (palette-cleansers between 6-7 min epics), trip-hop compact tracks. Compositional discipline = compositional power. Pair with `compact_aggressive_under_2_minutes` for even tighter punk-electronic statement, OR `compressed_economy_under_three_minutes_in_long_album` for diversity within longer-track albums.
compact_runtime_2_to_3_minutes.applicability_text: Album tracks that don't need to be singles; songs with limited harmonic content (compact length matches compact harmony); tracks meant as 'breath' between heavier album moments.
compact_runtime_2_to_3_minutes.trade_offs: Demands strong material — every section scrutinized. Risk of feeling like sketch rather than song if hook/identity weak.
compact_runtime_2_to_3_minutes.documented_example: Dumb at ~2:30 — Nirvana's compositional restraint demonstrating that short song length can serve a meditative aesthetic.

albini_mix_pure_capture_no_remix.rule: When mixing, REJECT the radio-commercial template. No master bus compression, no vocal compression beyond minimal taming, no added reverb beyond what the recording room provides. The mix IS the recording — no sweetening, no shaping. Trust the band + room + mics + performance.
albini_mix_pure_capture_no_remix.electronic_translation: Pure-capture electronic mix philosophy: NO master-bus compressor, NO master-bus EQ, NO multi-band glue, NO post-mix limiter beyond peak protection at -1 dBTP. Use only IN-TRACK processing during composition (synth-internal saturation, individual bus EQ to carve frequency space). Master peaks at -14 to -16 LUFS integrated — significantly under commercial loudness. Ambient + dub-techno + neo-classical-electronic releases benefit from this — listener attention rewards dynamic detail. The 'recording IS the mix': decisions made during synthesis/sampling stage cannot be undone in mix. Demands disciplined sound-design upstream. Apply for: ambient album releases targeting Bandcamp/vinyl, dub-techno 12-inches, neo-classical-electronic ECM-style albums, IDM auteur-fidelity statements (Aphex Twin Selected Ambient Works II tradition).
albini_mix_pure_capture_no_remix.applicability_text: Tracks where intimacy is the artistic goal; songs whose performance IS the production (no enhancement needed); intentionally raw/uncommercial moments on an album.
albini_mix_pure_capture_no_remix.trade_offs: Requires great recording room + great mics + great performance. If any of those are weak, the mix will expose them. Litt-style polish hides deficits; Albini-style capture amplifies them.
albini_mix_pure_capture_no_remix.documented_example: Dumb's mix preserves the band's room performance with no sweetening — vocal intimate and unprocessed, cello sits naturally in the band.

vocal_intimacy_without_rasp.rule: Deliver vocal at LOW intensity throughout. No screaming, no rasp escalation, no belted chorus. Pitch-stable, conversational, close-mic. The song's emotional weight comes from VOCAL RESTRAINT, not vocal exertion. Yorke-adjacent territory within rock context.
vocal_intimacy_without_rasp.electronic_translation: Track CLOSE-MIC vocal at low intensity (Neumann TLM 102 / U87 / cheaper SM7B at 4-6 inches). Vocalist whispers / speaks / sings just-above-conversation. Pitch-stable — no rasp, no air, no escalation. Mix processing: light compression (3-5 dB max with 4:1 ratio), de-esser, plate reverb @ 1.5-2.5s decay subtle send (-15 to -20 dB), maybe slap-delay 60-80ms at -25 dB for spatial depth. Apply for: trip-hop (Massive Attack, Portishead, Tricky tradition — whispered intimate vocals over heavy beats), downtempo introspective vocals, neo-classical-electronic art-song (Olafur Arnalds collabs), dark-electronic vulnerable moments (NIN 'The Day the World Went Away'). The compositional power is in restraint — the LACK of exertion makes listeners lean in.
vocal_intimacy_without_rasp.applicability_text: Sections requiring vulnerability; verses or bridges where vocal is exposed; songs centered on a single sustained emotional state.
vocal_intimacy_without_rasp.trade_offs: Demands vocalist with restraint capacity + close-mic technique. Risk of feeling underwhelming for listeners conditioned to vocal explosion.
vocal_intimacy_without_rasp.documented_example: Dumb's vocal — Cobain restrained throughout, no rasp escalation, the song's weight from his pitch-stable conversational delivery.

a_cappella_fakeout_intro_into_band_slam.rule: Open the song with a SINGLE VOCAL phrase, a cappella (no instruments), for 2-4 bars. Voice MUST be casual/restrained. Then ALL INSTRUMENTS enter SIMULTANEOUSLY at maximum density and intensity for the song's first beat after the vocal phrase. Listener's pattern-recognition is subverted — the casual vocal sets up an explosion they don't see coming.
a_cappella_fakeout_intro_into_band_slam.electronic_translation: Open with single dry SPOKEN/WHISPERED vocal phrase a cappella for 2-4 bars (no kick, no synth, just voice + light room reverb). Then on bar 3 or 5 — DROP: full kick + bass + saturated synth wall + snare pattern hit simultaneously. The casual-vocal-into-explosion pattern subverts listener expectation. Apply for: dark-electronic peak openers (NIN-adjacent), industrial-techno / EBM aggressive intros, dubstep drop-setup (different but related lineage), glitch-track rupture-openers. Use ONCE per album max — the surprise is single-use; listeners brace for it after first encounter. Variant: replace vocal with sample (movie-dialogue, found-recording, telephone-recording) for documentary-fakeout setup.
a_cappella_fakeout_intro_into_band_slam.applicability_text: Drop entries that must surprise (more impactful than interrupted_silence cadence at high tempos); songs that want to subvert listener pattern-recognition; tracks targeting hardcore-punk-influenced aesthetics.
a_cappella_fakeout_intro_into_band_slam.trade_offs: Use ONCE per song. The fakeout's surprise is single-use; if attempted again later, listener will brace for it. Demands committed casual delivery on the a cappella phrase.
a_cappella_fakeout_intro_into_band_slam.documented_example: Territorial Pissings — Krist Novoselic's a-cappella mock of 'Get Together' / 'Come on people now' followed by full-band slam at extreme tempo.

unrelenting_aggression_no_dynamic_arc.rule: After the initial slam, sustain MAXIMUM band density and intensity for the song's entire ~2 minutes. No bridge for breath. No chorus dynamic shift. No verse restraint. Sustained intensity with no pacing — listener is overwhelmed in compressed timeframe.
unrelenting_aggression_no_dynamic_arc.electronic_translation: After intro slam, sustain MAXIMUM density across full 2-minute runtime — no breakdown, no buildup, no breath. Full programmed kick + heavily distorted bass synth + percussive samples + screamed/saturated vocal-as-instrument all firing every bar. The listener can't catch breath; the track ends before fatigue sets in. Apply for: industrial-techno punishment tracks (Perc / Ancient Methods / Untold), EBM peak intensity (Front 242 short tracks), dark-electronic punk-energy bursts, gabber/hard-techno crossover. PAIR WITH compact_aggressive_under_2_minutes — the brevity is what makes the unrelenting-density bearable. Track length > 3 min destroys the effect (becomes monotonous).
unrelenting_aggression_no_dynamic_arc.applicability_text: Hardcore punk-influenced tracks; songs under 3 minutes; drops that prefer sustained intensity over peak-and-release.
unrelenting_aggression_no_dynamic_arc.trade_offs: Requires VERY short runtime (under 3 min). At 4+ minutes, the lack of variation becomes monotonous. Pair with COMPACT FORMAT recipe.
unrelenting_aggression_no_dynamic_arc.documented_example: Territorial Pissings — sustained max density across ~2:20, no bridge, no breath, no resolution.

abrupt_song_end_no_fade.rule: Cut the song mid-phrase or on the last beat of a chorus — NO fade-out, NO sustained final chord, NO resolution. The listener is left in silence after the song ends abruptly. Closure is denied as compositional choice.
abrupt_song_end_no_fade.electronic_translation: End the track on the LAST KICK HIT of a bar — no reverb tail, no decreasing layers, no fade automation, no master-volume ramp-down. Hard cut. Set Live's track end at the precise sample after the kick decay. Listener is left in silence, unsettled. Apply for: industrial-techno tracks (Surgeon / Regis hard-cut endings), dark-electronic punctuation tracks, EBM brutalism, glitch jolt-endings, IDM dialectical-statement endings. AVOID for ambient (incompatible — ambient demands reverb-tail resolution), deep house (DJ-needs-fade for mixing), dub-techno (echoing-tail tradition). Also possible: end on a single rendered click/glitch artifact — the digital equivalent of slamming a door.
