Apply for: neo-classical-electronic compositions with classical-tradition rubato-feel (Olafur Arnalds 'Sudden Throw' floating-tempo tradition), IDM with felt-rather-than-counted complexity (Aphex Twin 'Drukqs' tradition), trip-hop with jazz-tradition rhythmic-floating (Portishead 'Roads' floating-tempo), ambient with rhythmic foundation that doesn't lock (drone-techno ambiguous-meter), neo-classical-electronic intimate pieces with chamber-music phrasing. Different from `seven_four_asymmetric_meter_as_structural_device` (felt as STRUCTURAL device) — this is ambiguity-itself as the goal.
intentionally_ambiguous_rhythm_felt_not_.applicability_text: Compositions valuing felt-rather-than-counted rhythm; tracks with classical/jazz-tradition phrasing influence; productions willing to abandon strict-grid aesthetic.
intentionally_ambiguous_rhythm_felt_not_.trade_offs: Demands listener tolerance for floating-rhythm aesthetic. Risk of feeling shapeless if material weak. Won't work in dance-floor contexts.
intentionally_ambiguous_rhythm_felt_not_.documented_example: Pyramid Song (Radiohead, Amnesiac 2001, 4:49 in 6/4-or-8/8-felt-as-jazz-pulse) — listeners debate the meter's literal interpretation but feel the swing.

piano_composition_with_chromatic_harmony.rule: Compose track ON PIANO with deliberate CHROMATIC HARMONY (chord-progression includes chromatic passing-chords + non-diatonic chord choices). Piano as compositional source-instrument; chromatic motion as harmonic foundation. Demonstrates classical/jazz-tradition compositional ambition transferred to rock context.
piano_composition_with_chromatic_harmony.electronic_translation: Compose track ON ACTUAL PIANO (or weighted-keys MIDI controller for tactile feedback) using deliberate CHROMATIC HARMONY (chord-progression includes chromatic passing-chords like F#m → Fm → Em → Ebm descending chromatically, OR non-diatonic chord choices like Aeolian-with-borrowed-Phrygian-flat-2). The piano's TACTILE constraint shapes harmonic decisions differently than DAW-piano-roll composition. Translate the piano-composition into electronic context via Spitfire LABS Soft Piano + Diva pads voicing each chord-tone + sub-bass pedal. Apply for: neo-classical-electronic compositions with classical/jazz-harmonic ambition (Olafur Arnalds 'For Now I Am Winter' chromatic-piano tradition, Nils Frahm chromatic-piano works, Max Richter chromatic-classical compositions), trip-hop ballads with sophisticated harmony, ambient with chromatic-harmonic depth (Brian Eno + Harold Budd 'Plateaux of Mirror' chromatic-piano tradition), electronic-pop with classical-influenced harmony. Pair with `mingus_freedom_chromatic_harmony_inspiration` and `classical_influenced_chord_progression_in_electronic_context`.
piano_composition_with_chromatic_harmony.applicability_text: Compositions with piano-source + classical/jazz-harmonic ambition; productions valuing chromatic-harmony depth; tracks bridging classical-tradition + electronic-context.
piano_composition_with_chromatic_harmony.trade_offs: Demands piano + harmonic-theory literacy. Risk of feeling academic if execution lacks emotional weight.
piano_composition_with_chromatic_harmony.documented_example: Pyramid Song (Radiohead, Amnesiac 2001) — Yorke composed on piano with chromatic chord movement (F#m / Em / G / A / Bm / C / Dm / Em-with-modal-inflection). Documented influence: Charles Mingus 'Freedom'.

late_drum_entry_mid_song_for_perceptual_.rule: Drums DELAY their entry until mid-song (around 1:30-2:30 mark in a 4-5 min track). Listener experiences PRE-DRUMS section as 'floating piano-and-vocal,' POST-DRUMS as 'anchored composition' — perceptual shift mid-track. Different from intro-only delay (4-bar guitar-alone start) — this is STRUCTURAL MID-SONG SHIFT.
late_drum_entry_mid_song_for_perceptual_.electronic_translation: Drums DELAY their entry until MID-TRACK (around 1:30-2:30 mark in a 4-5 min electronic piece). Build pre-drums section from sustained pads + Rhodes/piano + vocal/lead — listener experiences this as FLOATING / RUBATO / NON-GRIDDED. Drums enter at structural mid-point — listener experiences post-drums as ANCHORED / GRIDDED / TIME-LOCKED. The PERCEPTUAL SHIFT mid-track is the compositional event. Apply for: neo-classical-electronic compositions with delayed-rhythm-section structural moment (Olafur Arnalds 'Near Light' tradition, Nils Frahm extended-builds), trip-hop emotional pieces (Massive Attack 'Teardrop' adjacent — sustained intro before rhythmic foundation enters), ambient-with-form pieces, downtempo emotional pieces, IDM compositions with late-drum-entry as compositional event. Different from `delayed_rhythm_section_entry_4_bars` (intro-only ~30 sec delay) — this is STRUCTURAL mid-song event around 50% of runtime. Pair with `selway_ambiguous_drum_phrasing_interpreting_meter_not_locking`.
late_drum_entry_mid_song_for_perceptual_.applicability_text: Compositions where pre-drums and post-drums sections create perceptual contrast; tracks valuing structural mid-song shift; productions willing to commit to extended pre-rhythm setup.
late_drum_entry_mid_song_for_perceptual_.trade_offs: Demands strong pre-drums material to hold attention. Risk of feeling sectional if shift not earned compositionally.
late_drum_entry_mid_song_for_perceptual_.documented_example: Pyramid Song — drums (Phil Selway) enter at 1:50 in 4:49 song. Pre-drums = piano + vocal floating; post-drums = ambiguously-anchored composition.

ondes_martenot_or_rare_classical_instrum.rule: Use RARE CLASSICAL/EARLY-ELECTRONIC INSTRUMENT (ondes martenot, theremin, glass harmonica, mellotron, etc.) for SPECTRAL COLOR not available from standard instruments. Rare instrument as SIGNATURE TIMBRE-ELEMENT recurring across catalog. Documented as Greenwood's ondes martenot tradition for Radiohead.
ondes_martenot_or_rare_classical_instrum.electronic_translation: Use RARE CLASSICAL/EARLY-ELECTRONIC INSTRUMENT samples (Spitfire LABS Ondes Martenot, Spitfire BBCSO Ondes / Theremin, Output Substance for esoteric textures, Native Instruments Glass Harmonica from Komplete, mellotron-emulation via UVI Mello or M-Tron Pro) for SPECTRAL COLOR not available from standard synth-design or piano. The rare-instrument's SIGNATURE TIMBRE recurring across multiple tracks of an album = artist's identity-anchor. Apply for: neo-classical-electronic with signature-rare-instrument tradition (Olafur Arnalds with chamber-organ tradition, Nils Frahm with prepared-piano), ambient with rare-instrument color (Eno + ambient tradition with mellotron / strange acoustic instruments), trip-hop with featured-rare-instrument (Massive Attack with featured ondes-martenot-adjacent textures), IDM with esoteric instrument-sample integration. The DISCIPLINE: choose ONE rare-instrument as your signature; deploy across multiple album-tracks to establish identity-thread; resist using it as gimmick. Pair with `external_contributor_for_distinct_dimensional_color`.
ondes_martenot_or_rare_classical_instrum.applicability_text: Productions seeking distinctive timbre identity; multi-album catalogs with signature-instrument tradition; tracks valuing spectral-color beyond standard synth-design.
ondes_martenot_or_rare_classical_instrum.trade_offs: Demands access to rare instrument or quality emulation. Risk of feeling gimmicky if not integrated compositionally.
ondes_martenot_or_rare_classical_instrum.documented_example: Greenwood's ondes martenot (1928 French electronic-classical instrument) used across multiple Radiohead pieces, including Pyramid Song. Signature timbre-element of Radiohead's post-OK-Computer catalog.

mingus_freedom_chromatic_harmony_inspira.rule: Document specific compositional INSPIRATION SOURCE (e.g., Charles Mingus 'Freedom' for chromatic harmony) and let the influence be AUDIBLE in the track. Naming the inspiration source LEGITIMIZES harmonic ambition + invites listener-curiosity. Different from generic 'jazz-influenced' — specific reference is more powerful.
mingus_freedom_chromatic_harmony_inspira.electronic_translation: Document a SPECIFIC COMPOSITIONAL INSPIRATION SOURCE (a particular jazz piece, classical composition, electronic-music landmark) and let the influence be AUDIBLE in the track. Name the source in interviews/press materials/liner notes. The naming LEGITIMIZES your harmonic/structural/textural ambition + INVITES LISTENER-CURIOSITY toward source material. Apply for: neo-classical-electronic with specific-classical-piece inspirations (Olafur Arnalds citing specific chamber-music influences, Max Richter explicitly building on Vivaldi tradition), trip-hop with specific-jazz inspirations, IDM with specific-electronic-music landmarks (Aphex Twin citing Stockhausen, Erik Satie), ambient with specific-Eno-piece references, dark-electronic with specific-influence chains. The DISCIPLINE: the influence must be AUDIBLE not merely claimed; the source must be LEGITIMATELY RELATED to your composition; the citation respects the source rather than parodying it. Pair with `piano_composition_with_chromatic_harmony` and `classical_influenced_chord_progression_in_electronic_context`.
mingus_freedom_chromatic_harmony_inspira.applicability_text: Compositions with specific documented influence-source; productions valuing transparent inspiration-citation; tracks bridging genre-traditions via explicit reference.
mingus_freedom_chromatic_harmony_inspira.trade_offs: Demands legitimate familiarity with source material. Risk of feeling pretentious if citation isn't earned by composition's depth.
mingus_freedom_chromatic_harmony_inspira.documented_example: Yorke documented Pyramid Song as inspired by Charles Mingus's 'Freedom' chromatic harmony. Influence audible in chord movement; citation legitimizes the harmonic ambition.

funeral_march_tempo_70_bpm_for_solemnity.rule: Set tempo around FUNERAL-MARCH RANGE (60-80 BPM) for emotional weight + solemnity. Slow tempo + sustained chord-changes + careful phrasing = compositional WEIGHT impossible at faster tempos. Reject the modern-music-default 100+ BPM expectation.
funeral_march_tempo_70_bpm_for_solemnity.electronic_translation: Set tempo around FUNERAL-MARCH RANGE (60-80 BPM) for compositions where EMOTIONAL WEIGHT + SOLEMNITY are the goals. Slow tempo + sustained chord-changes + careful phrasing produces compositional WEIGHT impossible at faster tempos. Reject the dance-floor-default 120-130 BPM expectation. Apply on Sub 37 / Diva for sustained low-end pad-foundation, Spitfire LABS Soft Piano for chamber-tradition piano-foundation, processed-vocal as foreground. Apply for: ambient pieces (slow-tempo IS the genre — Stars of the Lid 'And Their Refinement of the Decline'-tradition near-static tempo), neo-classical-electronic emotional pieces (Olafur Arnalds 'Sudden Throw' funeral-march-territory), trip-hop ballads (Portishead 'Roads' slow-tempo emotional weight), downtempo evening-listening pieces (60-80 BPM tradition), drone-techno meditative tracks. Different from techno default-tempos (130+ BPM) — this REJECTS the dance-floor BPM range deliberately.
funeral_march_tempo_70_bpm_for_solemnity.applicability_text: Compositions valuing emotional weight + solemnity; productions willing to commit to slow-tempo aesthetic; tracks rejecting modern-music BPM defaults.
funeral_march_tempo_70_bpm_for_solemnity.trade_offs: Demands listener tolerance for slow-tempo. Won't work for dance-floor or radio contexts.
funeral_march_tempo_70_bpm_for_solemnity.documented_example: Pyramid Song (Radiohead, Amnesiac 2001, ~70 BPM funeral-march range) — solemnity + emotional weight from slow tempo + sustained chord changes.

selway_ambiguous_drum_phrasing_interpret.rule: Drummer INTERPRETS ambiguous meter via PHRASING that EVOKES feel without LOCKING listener to grid. Different from drum-as-timekeeper (locks listener) and from free-improv drumming (no timing reference) — this is INTERPRETATIVE drumming that supports floating-meter without resolving its ambiguity.
selway_ambiguous_drum_phrasing_interpret.electronic_translation: Program drums (or direct live-drummer guest) to INTERPRET an ambiguous meter via PHRASING that supports the float without locking the listener to a specific grid-count. Use HUMANIZED VELOCITY VARIATION (random ±15%), MICRO-TIMING NUDGES (-15ms to +15ms per hit), VARIABLE QUANTIZE STRENGTH (60-80% per hit), GHOST-NOTES on snare for textural-rhythm-complexity. The drum-bus EVOKES feel without resolving ambiguity. Apply for: neo-classical-electronic with chamber-percussion phrasing (Olafur Arnalds + Joey Castillo collaboration tradition), trip-hop with floating-rhythm drum-bus (Portishead 'Roads' interpretive-drumming tradition), ambient with rhythmic-foundation that breathes, IDM with rubato-feel programmed-drums, downtempo with floating-tempo drum-bus. Demands either skilled live-drummer guest OR sophisticated programming with humanization-discipline. Pair with `intentionally_ambiguous_rhythm_felt_not_counted` and `late_drum_entry_mid_song_for_perceptual_shift`.
selway_ambiguous_drum_phrasing_interpret.applicability_text: Compositions with floating/ambiguous-meter; productions valuing interpretative drumming; tracks where drum-bus supports float without resolving it.
selway_ambiguous_drum_phrasing_interpret.trade_offs: Demands drummer with interpretative musicianship + restraint OR sophisticated humanization programming. Risk of feeling shapeless if execution off.
selway_ambiguous_drum_phrasing_interpret.documented_example: Phil Selway's drumming on Pyramid Song — entering at 1:50 with phrasing that interprets the 6/4-or-8/8 meter without locking listeners to a strict grid count.

broken_metallic_percussion_loop_as_compo.rule: Build composition on DISTINCTIVE BROKEN-METALLIC PERCUSSION LOOP — found-object percussion, processed metallic-sample, or programmed-glitch-percussion at the FOUNDATION of arrangement. Loop has identifiable sonic character (broken/clattering/metallic) different from standard kit. Rest of arrangement built around the loop.
broken_metallic_percussion_loop_as_compo.electronic_translation: Build composition on DISTINCTIVE BROKEN-METALLIC PERCUSSION LOOP at arrangement foundation. Sources: found-object recordings (metal-pipe hits, factory-machine clatter, industrial debris), processed industrial-percussion samples (Iceworks Vol. 1, Spitfire Percussion + heavy distortion + bit-crush), programmed-glitch percussion (Reaktor Razor patches, Oval-tradition CD-corruption-derived percussion, granular-percussion via Granulator II on metallic source-samples). The loop has IDENTIFIABLE BROKEN/CLATTERING/METALLIC sonic character DIFFERENT from standard 808/909 kit. Rest of arrangement (bass, lead-melody, vocal/lead-elements) built AROUND the loop's groove + character. Apply for: glitch compositions (FUNDAMENTAL — Oval / Pole / Vladislav Delay tradition built on broken/processed-percussion foundations), IDM with industrial-percussion identity (Aphex Twin 'Drukqs' broken-percussion tradition), industrial-techno with metallic-percussion identity (Vatican Shadow / Ancient Methods tradition), dark-electronic with found-percussion foundation, dub-techno with industrial-percussion-color (Pole 'Fragmented' tradition). Pair with `programmed_drums_as_compositional_engine`.
broken_metallic_percussion_loop_as_compo.applicability_text: Compositions valuing distinctive percussion-character; productions with found-object recording capacity or sophisticated sample-processing; tracks where percussion-loop is foundation, not background.
broken_metallic_percussion_loop_as_compo.trade_offs: Demands distinctive-percussion sourcing or sample-design fluency. Risk of feeling gimmicky if loop-character is forced rather than emergent.
broken_metallic_percussion_loop_as_compo.documented_example: Packt Like Sardines in a Crushd Tin Box (Radiohead, Amnesiac 2001) — broken metallic percussion loop opens album as compositional foundation. Documented 'snake-charmer'-adjacent textural identity.

pitch_shifted_vocal_as_compositional_ele.rule: Apply PITCH-SHIFTING (formant + pitch manipulation) to vocal as COMPOSITIONAL ELEMENT — preserves vocalist's identifiable voice but shifts register/character. Different from vocoded vocal (masks idiosyncrasies) — pitch-shifted vocal RETAINS the singer's grain while transforming pitch.
pitch_shifted_vocal_as_compositional_ele.electronic_translation: Apply PITCH-SHIFTING to vocal — formant-corrected pitch shift (Soundtoys Crystallizer, Ableton's Pitch utility, Logic's Pitch-Shifter II, hardware Eventide H3000) shifts the register up/down 6-12 semitones while PRESERVING the singer's identifiable vocal grain. Different from vocoded-vocal (which masks delivery via carrier-modulation) — pitch-shifted vocal RETAINS the singer's character while transforming pitch-register. Apply on the lead vocal (one octave down for sub-baritone gravity, one octave up for ethereal-falsetto effect, formant-shifted up but pitch-stable for chipmunk-voice character). Apply for: glitch with pitch-shifted vocal-fragments (Oval / RJD2 tradition), IDM with pitch-manipulated vocal-as-instrument (Aphex Twin 'Come To Daddy' processed-vocal tradition), trip-hop with pitch-shifted vocal-color (Burial 'Untrue' tradition with pitch-down vocals), dark-electronic with sub-baritone shifted-vocal, electronic-pop with featured pitch-shift moments. Different from `manipulated_vocal_samples_as_textural_color` (broader umbrella including granular + spectral) — this is specifically the pitch-shift technique. Pair with `manipulated_vocal_samples_as_textural_color`.
pitch_shifted_vocal_as_compositional_ele.applicability_text: Compositions valuing vocal pitch-character manipulation; productions with pitch-shifting tools; tracks where vocal-as-compositional-color matters more than vocal-as-narrative.
pitch_shifted_vocal_as_compositional_ele.trade_offs: Risk of vocal becoming unrecognizable if shift too aggressive. Demands pitch-shifting tools + judgment about appropriate shift amount.
pitch_shifted_vocal_as_compositional_ele.documented_example: Packt Like Sardines (Radiohead, Amnesiac 2001) — Yorke's vocal pitch-shifted as compositional element while retaining his recognizable vocal-grain.

album_opener_via_textural_disorientation.rule: OPEN ALBUM with TEXTURAL DISORIENTATION — broken percussion + unusual sonic palette + non-rock instrumentation as immediate listener-recalibration. Different from `complete_genre_reinvention_within_band_catalog` (album-level statement) — this is the OPENER's STRUCTURAL FUNCTION within the reinvention, immediately repositioning listener expectations from bar 1.
album_opener_via_textural_disorientation.electronic_translation: OPEN ALBUM/EP with TEXTURAL DISORIENTATION — broken/unusual percussion foundation + unusual sonic palette + non-conventional instrumentation immediately RECALIBRATES listener expectations from bar 1. The opener's structural function = MARK THE NEW TERRITORY for the album/EP. Apply on Granulator II for granular-vocal textures, Reaktor blocks for esoteric synthesis, found-percussion samples for foundation, processed lead-elements for foreground. Apply for: ambient album-openers with disorienting sonic palette (Tim Hecker 'Ravedeath, 1972' tradition), IDM concept-album openers (Aphex Twin 'Drukqs' opener tradition with corrupted-textures), dark-electronic concept-album openers, glitch album-openers (Oval / Pole tradition), electronic-experimental EP openers, neo-classical-electronic openers with jarring-piece (challenging classical-listener-expectations). Different from `asymmetric_meter_as_album_opener_statement_of_intent` (rhythmic-statement opener) — this is TEXTURAL-statement opener. Pair with `complete_genre_reinvention_within_band_catalog`.
album_opener_via_textural_disorientation.applicability_text: Album openers wanting immediate listener-recalibration; multi-track concept albums valuing strong opener-statement; productions willing to challenge listeners from bar 1.
album_opener_via_textural_disorientation.trade_offs: Risk of alienating listeners who skip the opener. Demands strong opening material that earns the disorientation.
album_opener_via_textural_disorientation.documented_example: Packt Like Sardines as Amnesiac opener (Radiohead 2001) — broken metallic percussion + pitch-shifted vocal opens album, immediately repositioning listener expectations after Kid A.

production_philosophy_signature_godrich_.rule: DEPLOY same engineer-producer-mixer-with-band-partnership framework across MULTIPLE ALBUMS that explore DIFFERENT compositional vocabularies. Producer's range demonstrated by sustaining same partnership-philosophy across genre-experimentation. Long-term producer-band relationship enables multi-album evolution.
production_philosophy_signature_godrich_.electronic_translation: DEPLOY the same producer-engineer-mixer + integrated-creative-partnership framework across MULTIPLE ALBUMS that explore DIFFERENT compositional vocabularies. Album N: techno-experimental palette with producer-collaborator. Album N+1: ambient palette with SAME producer. Album N+2: neo-classical-electronic palette with SAME producer. Producer's RANGE demonstrated by sustaining the same partnership-philosophy across multi-album genre-experimentation. Long-term producer-collaborator relationship ENABLES multi-album evolution rather than constraining it. Apply for: established electronic-artist + producer-collaborator multi-album arcs (Aphex Twin + Mike Marsh mastering tradition, Olafur Arnalds + various-producer multi-album evolution), neo-classical-electronic projects with sustained producer-relationship across stylistic shifts, ambient projects with deep producer-partnership across catalogs, techno-experimental artists with named producer across multiple releases. The DISCIPLINE: pick a producer whose RANGE matches your evolutionary ambitions; commit to multi-album partnership rather than single-album hire. Pair with `auteur_framework_evolution_across_decades_with_retained_identity` and `godrich_engineer_producer_mixer_unified_role_with_band_partnership`.
production_philosophy_signature_godrich_.applicability_text: Multi-album electronic projects with sustained producer-partnership; productions valuing producer-as-evolutionary-partner; tracks within multi-album-arc context.
production_philosophy_signature_godrich_.trade_offs: Demands sustained producer-collaborator availability + creative compatibility across multiple albums. Risk of partnership stagnation if both parties don't evolve.
production_philosophy_signature_godrich_.documented_example: Nigel Godrich's continuation across Radiohead's Kid A (2000) + Amnesiac (2001) — same producer-engineer-mixer-with-band-partnership framework deployed for both Kid A's electronic-experimental palette and Amnesiac's broken-metallic / pitch-shifted-vocal evolution.

lenses._note: Pointer collections — each lens key is an axis value, each value is a sorted list of recipe IDs. No data duplication.

default_blend_projection._note: Top 30 recipes ranked by fit_score for the documented default personality blend (Cobain 30 / Reznor 30 / Daft Punk 25 / Yorke 15). Score = sum(blend_pct * personality_fit_breakdown_pct) / 10000, normalized to 0.0-1.0. Tiebreaker: wow_factor descending. Use top_30 as starting compositional palette when blend matches user default; recompute via fit_score_formula for custom blends.
default_blend_projection.fit_score_formula: fit_score = sum(blend_vector[k] * recipe.personality_fit_breakdown[k] for k in [cobain, reznor, daft_punk, yorke]) / 10000.0  # normalized to 0.0-1.0 range
default_blend_projection.ranking_secondary_key: wow_factor (descending) — used as tiebreaker when fit_scores within 0.01 of each other

synergy_clusters.evidence_rule: A cluster is valid if its recipes share documented compositional context — same song.advisor_extract (song_cluster) or same production_philosophy_lineage (philosophy_cluster).

conflict_pairs._note: Logical conflict pairs from each atom's conflict_recipes field. 261 unique pairs, 56 mutually documented (both atoms cite each other).
conflict_pairs.logical_conflict_rule: Two recipes conflict if their rule premises contain mutually exclusive constraints. Mutually-documented pairs are higher-confidence; unilateral pairs are still valid.

pairs.rule_a_premise: Open the song with a SINGLE VOCAL phrase, a cappella (no instruments), for 2-4 bars

composition_path_templates._note: 8 self-contained composition path templates spanning composition→arrangement→performance→recording→mixing→mastering. Recipe IDs are pointer-references — no data duplication.

template_schema.name: snake_case template id
template_schema.next_stage_recipes: Object keyed by stage (arrangement/performance/recording/mixing/mastering), each value an array of recipe ids
template_schema.wow_factor_total: Sum of wow_factors of all included recipes (rough quality budget)
template_schema.documented_song_inspirations: Array of source songs whose composition the template generalizes
template_schema.trade_offs: String describing what the template DEMANDS of the user (gear, skills, runtime, audience tolerance)

industrial_techno_aggression_track.name: industrial_techno_aggression_track
industrial_techno_aggression_track.description: Compressed-economy aggressive dark/industrial techno track (140-150 BPM, 3-4 min, max-density). Punishment-floor DJ-tool aesthetic with Reznor-Cobain compositional pull and Surgeon/Perc/Charlotte-de-Witte production lineage.
industrial_techno_aggression_track.trade_offs: Demands aggressive sound-design fluency, mix-bus saturation chain (Decapitator + Vari-Mu), commitment to hard-techno aesthetic.

filter_house_extended_dance_track.name: filter_house_extended_dance_track
filter_house_extended_dance_track.description: Extended-form (5-9 min) filter-house DJ track at 120-128 BPM with progressive-layering arrangement, vocoded-vocal hook, filter-modulation as compositional engine. Daft Punk Homework / Around the World tradition.
documented_song_inspirations: Daft Punk Harder Better Faster Stronger
filter_house_extended_dance_track.trade_offs: Demands TR-909 access (or quality emulation) + analog-modeled filter (Diva/Cytomic) + vocoder chain.

ambient_atmospheric_meditation_piece.name: ambient_atmospheric_meditation_piece
ambient_atmospheric_meditation_piece.description: Extended-form (6-10+ min) atmospheric ambient piece. Drone-foundation + sustained-pad layers + minimum-element-count + atmospheric-continuity over dynamic-contrast. Eno / Stars of the Lid / Olafur Arnalds tradition.
documented_song_inspirations: NIN The Day The World Went Away
ambient_atmospheric_meditation_piece.trade_offs: Demands restraint, patience, mature compositional voice. -14 LUFS master target.

electronic_pop_anthemic_single.name: electronic_pop_anthemic_single
electronic_pop_anthemic_single.description: Pop-format (3:30-4:00) electronic single with verse-chorus structure, vocoded-or-clean lead, anthemic chorus density. Daft Punk Discovery / Justice / Madeon tradition.
documented_song_inspirations: Daft Punk Harder Better Faster Stronger
electronic_pop_anthemic_single.trade_offs: Demands hook-craft + accessible song-form. -7 LUFS playlist-loudness master.

dark_electronic_hybrid_album_centerpiece.name: dark_electronic_hybrid_album_centerpiece
dark_electronic_hybrid_album_centerpiece.description: Extended-form (6-9 min) dark-electronic concept-album centerpiece with hybrid-vocabulary integration, vocal-hybrid delivery, section-specific processing. NIN The Fragile / Wretched tradition.
documented_song_inspirations: NIN The Day The World Went Away
dark_electronic_hybrid_album_centerpiece.trade_offs: Demands sophisticated arrangement + section-specific processing chains + extensive automation.

idm_rhythm_design_showpiece.name: idm_rhythm_design_showpiece
idm_rhythm_design_showpiece.description: Mid-form (4-7 min) IDM/glitch composition where rhythm-design IS the spotlight. Programmed-drums-as-engine, asymmetric meter, intricate percussion-layer choreography. Aphex Twin / Autechre / Squarepusher tradition.
documented_song_inspirations: QOTSA A Song for the Dead
idm_rhythm_design_showpiece.trade_offs: Demands advanced drum-programming + sample-design fluency + asymmetric-meter composition.

trip_hop_ballad_emotional_centerpiece.name: trip_hop_ballad_emotional_centerpiece
trip_hop_ballad_emotional_centerpiece.description: Mid-form (4-6 min) trip-hop / neo-classical-electronic ballad with vulnerable-vocal intimacy, Rhodes-piano foundation, sustained-pad atmosphere, gradual subtle build. Massive Attack Teardrop / Nirvana All Apologies / NIN The Day The World Went Away / Radiohead Pyramid Song tradition.
documented_song_inspirations: NIN The Day The World Went Away
trip_hop_ballad_emotional_centerpiece.trade_offs: Demands available vulnerable vocalist + restraint + intimate recording environment.

dub_techno_extended_meditation_track.name: dub_techno_extended_meditation_track
dub_techno_extended_meditation_track.description: Extended-form (7-12+ min) dub-techno meditation with drone-foundation, modal pad voicings above pedal, micro-variation hypnotic loops, extreme spatial-mix preservation. Basic Channel / Pole / Vladislav Delay tradition.
documented_song_inspirations: NIN The Day The World Went Away
dub_techno_extended_meditation_track.trade_offs: Demands deep low-end clarity discipline (60-130 Hz frequency-slot management), patience, dynamic-preservation mastering.
